jobs.title: Engineering Manager - Security Incident Response (EMEA)
jobs.description: <div class="tw-px-4 tw-py-2 tablet:tw-mx-auto tablet:tw-max-w-3xl tw-mb-10"> <div class="job-description"> <p>The Security Incident Response team is part of our Resilience Engineering organisation and plays a vital role in keeping Datadog safe. Our goal is to ensure that Datadog is prepared for and efficiently responds to security-related incidents, ensuring that threats to our systems and data are contained as fast as possible. We also partner with teams after incidents by leveraging them as opportunities to learn. By focusing on our ability to adapt and fix systemic problems, we contribute towards a larger culture of building resilience in our people and systems.</p> <p>As an Engineering Manager, you will help us realize this mission by leading a talented group of engineers who are committed to driving Datadog’s incident response capabilities to the next level. Along with building tools and automation to streamline our efficiency, you will work with key stakeholders across Datadog to ensure we are focusing our efforts in the right areas and are measuring how we improve. As part of a leadership team, you will be active in shaping our organizational strategy and culture.&nbsp;</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong><em>What You’ll Do:&nbsp;</em></strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead and mentor a team of experienced incident responders who are passionate about building a culture of security and resilience at Datadog. Help engineers grow to the next level and continuously provide them opportunities to develop.&nbsp;</li> <li>Serve as a hands-on leader during incidents. Lead under pressure, make decisions in ambiguous situations, and collaborate across several teams to drive towards resolution. Be on-call in our secondary rotation (along with around 5 other leaders), which is escalated to when responders need help with resourcing or decision-making.</li> <li>Ensure the team is triaging alerts and signals in Datadog Cloud SIEM consistently and to a high level so that we can respond to emerging threats. Partner with our Threat Detection team to tune and calibrate these signals so they’re delivering value.</li> <li>Build tools, systems, and processes to ensure Datadog is maturing its security incident response capabilities. Ensure that our operational capabilities are measured and communicated with stakeholders.</li> <li>Lead post-incident analysis efforts so that engineers at Datadog learn from security incidents, ensuring postmortems are blameless and actionable. Ensure we are capturing follow-up items that repair systematic issues and prevent repeated patterns.</li> <li>Partner with Datadog’s product management team by providing feedback on features and be willing to champion areas for improvement. Work with other security teams within Datadog to develop strategies and plans to prevent future security incidents.</li> </ul> <p><strong><em>Who You Are:&nbsp;</em></strong></p> <ul> <li>2-3 years experience as a people manager or as a technical leader with strong mentorship skills. Ideally candidates will have experience in career development, performance management, tracking and optimizing team velocity, OKRs, and hiring&nbsp; candidates into diverse and inclusive organizations.</li> <li>3-5 years experience working in incident response teams and large-scale incident response programs, with particular focus on security-related incidents and threats. Strong ownership and an ability to work outside your comfort zones to return Datadog to a stable state when failure does occur, even if it falls outside team boundaries.</li> <li>Technical pragmatism and an ability to help the team reason about trade-offs around implementation. You will often review the decisions and RFCs from senior engineers, and you will need to blend both your technical and business acumen to do this.</li> <li>Strong distributed systems knowledge. While we are not in the critical path for customer-facing infrastructure, we support people who are, so we need to leverage our technical experience to build credibility. We frequently read postmortems and offer suggestions, so you will need a good technical baseline.</li> <li>Strong stakeholder management skills. This will involve using your empathy, collaboration, and communication skills in English to work remotely with people across teams. Because our programs touch lots of different parts of the business, we frequently collaborate with stakeholders across Datadog and need to motivate teams to work together towards a shared goal.</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing</li> <li>Intradepartmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, ability to join our Community Guilds (Datadog employee resource groups)</li> <li>Access to Inclusion Talks, our internal panel discussions</li> <li>Free, global mental health benefits for employees and dependents age 6+</li> <li>Competitive global benefits</li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <div class="content-pay-transparency"> <div class="pay-input"> <div class="description"> <p>Datadog offers a competitive salary and equity package, and may include variable compensation. Actual compensation is based on factors such as the candidate's skills, qualifications, and experience. In addition, Datadog offers a wide range of best in class, comprehensive and inclusive employee benefits for this role including healthcare, dental, parental planning, and mental health benefits, a 401(k) plan and match, paid time off, fitness reimbursements, and a discounted employee stock purchase plan.</p> </div> <div class="pay-range">&nbsp;</div> </div> </div> <div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about #DatadogLife on&nbsp;<a href="https://www.instagram.com/datadoghq/?hl=en" target="_blank">Instagram</a>,&nbsp;<a href="https://www.linkedin.com/company/datadog" target="_blank">LinkedIn,</a>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<a href="https://learn.datadoghq.com/" target="_blank">Datadog Learning Center.</a></p> <hr> <p data-renderer-start-pos="215"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Equal Opportunity at Datadog:</strong></p> <p>Datadog is proud to offer&nbsp;<a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf" target="_blank">equal employment opportunity</a>&nbsp;to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and other characteristics protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Here are our&nbsp;<a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/candidate-legal-notices/" target="_blank">Candidate Legal Notices</a>&nbsp;for your reference.&nbsp;</p> <p>Datadog endeavors to make our Careers Page accessible to all users. If you would like to contact us regarding the accessibility of our website or need assistance completing the application process, please complete&nbsp;<a href="https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeoJeduEZ2NdZF_65uZmWUrCM-zee7yADXX1s6Xr9zAGq8iKA/viewform" target="_blank">this form</a>. This form is for accommodation requests only and cannot be used to inquire about the status of applications.&nbsp;</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Privacy and AI Guidelines:</strong></p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662">Any information you submit to Datadog as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Datadog’s&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" data-sk="tooltip_parent">Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice</a>. For information on our AI policy, please visit&nbsp;<a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/interviewing-at-datadog-ai-guidelines/" target="_blank">Interviewing at Datadog AI Guidelines</a>.</p> </div> <p class="tw-pt-8">&nbsp;</p> </div> </div> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. jobs.title: Front Desk Associate - Dublin
jobs.description: <p>At Datadog, Office Operations run the day-to-day operations for Datadog and help to keep our employees safe, happy and productive, which means every day is different. &nbsp;Office Operations work closely with leadership and employees to ensure that Datadog keeps scaling smoothly and remains a great place to work.</p> <p>As the Front Desk Associate at Datadog, you’ll be responsible for managing the front desk guest experience.&nbsp; You’ll assist with various vendor, event or product research efforts and will be a general go-to for the Dublin Datadog office questions for both visitors and employees alike.&nbsp; We are looking for a high-energy, “get it done” person who wants to learn and grow with the company as we rapidly expand.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Be a Brand Ambassador for the office, leaving employees, visitors and potential candidates with a positive impression of Datadog</li> <li>Own the reception area and set it up for success</li> <li>Be the main contact with our building management’s security partner in order to manage, coordinate and centralize all our badging requests&nbsp;</li> <li>Coordinate certificate of insurance requirements with vendors&nbsp;</li> <li>Create and distribute Datadog staff ID badges</li> <li>Play a key role in planning and executing monthly Datadog social events</li> <li>Research options for Datadog projects and events</li> <li>Work closely on maintaining a great relationship with our existing vendors and source new vendors for various requirements</li> <li>Audit and order office supplies/Manage shipping and receiving</li> <li>Provide exceptional customer service for all staff requests</li> <li>Manage overall aesthetic and cleanliness of reception area and mailroom</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Must have 1-3 years administrative / office&nbsp; experience, with excellent verbal and written communication skills</li> <li>Able to provide high-quality customer service</li> <li>Must have a friendly and positive demeanor</li> <li>Be adaptable to quick changes and growth</li> <li>Quick problem-solving skills, and the ability to stay calm under pressure</li> <li>Accuracy and attention to detail</li> <li>Willing to work occasional overtime</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. jobs.title: Manager I, Engineering - APM Backend
jobs.description: <p>APM Data Hub is at the core of APM, one of the product pillars of Datadog. Our team is responsible for the backend pipeline that processes, transforms, and routes tracing data at scale after ingestion. Our mission is to extend the pipeline's capabilities so that multiple products across Datadog can leverage the rich data present in traces — serving not only APM Distributed Tracing but a growing number of product teams that depend on this data.</p> <p>As an Engineering Manager for APM Data Hub, you'll lead a distributed team of 6 engineers with diverse seniority, shaping the technical vision of a platform that sits at a critical intersection of Datadog's product ecosystem. You'll own the roadmap for how tracing data is processed and exposed to multiple consumers, maintain high engineering standards for a system operating reliably at scale, and facilitate cross-functional collaboration with the product teams building on top of your&nbsp; pipeline. You'll have the opportunity to contribute to solving complex engineering challenges with a transversal impact on multiple products.<br><br></p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships that it builds, the creativity it brings to the table, and the collaboration of being together. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our employees can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Manage a team of 6 engineers responsible for a pipeline powering critical features of Datadog platform</li> <li>Provide technical leadership by identifying, addressing, and removing roadblocks to maintain team velocity and code quality.</li> <li>Collaborate closely with the Product teams to enhance our APM experience and with Platform teams to support our evolutions</li> <li>Lead strategic, multi-quarter initiatives, navigating ambiguity to establish clear goals and execution paths.</li> <li>Foster professional growth and career advancement for team members, including performance reviews and promotions.</li> <li>Participate and lead by example in the team's 24/7 on-call rotation to maintain service reliability.<br><br></li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Proven experience (1+ years) leading a team of 4+ engineers with an emphasis on customer-driven improvements.</li> <li>Skilled collaborator, able to effectively coordinate efforts across multiple teams and functions.</li> <li>Demonstrated technical judgment and ability to guide technical decisions across back-end distributed systems</li> <li>Passionate about mentoring and growing engineers, actively developing the next generation of technical leaders.</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. jobs.title: Manager I, Engineering - Code Coverage
jobs.description: <p>Datadog is looking for an Engineering Manager to lead and grow our Code Coverage team, which is building the next generation of AI-powered developer tooling. This team owns Datadog’s Code Coverage product across the entire stack, helping customers track and enforce test coverage. Code Coverage is part of the Software Delivery suite, which enables engineering teams to move faster and more securely.</p> <p>In this role, you will lead and develop a high-performing engineering team in an ambiguous environment. You’ll set technical direction, drive execution, and remain hands-on. You will partner closely with customers and product management to evolve the product into an automated system that leverages production signals and AI to improve test quality, relevance, and performance.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships that it builds, the creativity it brings to the table, and the collaboration of being together. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our employees can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead and develop a team of four engineers in Madrid, supporting their growth through mentorship, regular 1:1s, and clear performance feedback, while fostering a strong culture of ownership and quality</li> <li>Guide the evolution of Code Coverage into an AI-powered system that uses production data and LLMs to identify coverage gaps and improve test effectiveness</li> <li>Partner with product management and customers to define and evolve the roadmap, aligning stakeholders and translating strategy into clear priorities</li> <li>Stay hands-on by contributing to architecture and design decisions, and by participating in the on-call rotation<br><br></li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>You have a strong interest in AI and agentic engineering, and are curious about how LLMs and autonomous systems can improve developer workflows</li> <li>You have experience leading an engineering team as a tech lead or manager, with a track record of developing engineers at different levels</li> <li>You are an excellent leader with strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to build and lead high-performing teams and set priorities effectively</li> <li>You are comfortable operating in ambiguity and can drive execution in evolving environments by forming clear value hypotheses and iterating quickly</li> <li>You have hands-on experience with Java or Go, worked on large scale distributed systems, and are willing to stay close to the code and participate in an on-call rotation</li> <li>You have strong product sense and communication skills, and can collaborate effectively with product managers, customers, and cross-functional stakeholders</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. jobs.title: Manager I, Engineering - CodeGen
jobs.description: <p>Datadog’s CodeGen team builds systems that use AI to <strong>read, understand, generate, and safely change real code</strong> — powering automated fixes, CI/PR automation, agentic remediation, and Infrastructure-as-Code workflows. The team ties code understanding to observability so assistants can explain problems and produce safe, auditable code changes.<br><br></p> <p>We’re a new team building AI-assisted tools to make Datadog developers more effective, by autonomously generating tests, fixing bugs, and improving performance.</p> <p><br>We’re looking for a product-minded generalist to help us quickly define and ship products that make all Datadog customers 10x developers.<br><br></p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead a team of engineers responsible for building production systems that enable code understanding and automated code changes — from parsers and telemetry ingestion to model serving, evaluation, and PR automation.</li> <li>Drive technical direction and execution: set a clear roadmap, prioritize work, remove blockers, and raise quality and reliability bar for codegen features (safety, correctness, performance).</li> <li>Partner with product, applied research, infra/SRE, security, and other engineering teams to ship end-to-end experiences that safely apply model outputs (PRs, CI checks, automated apply flows).</li> <li>Build and maintain robust evaluation and monitoring pipelines (offline and online) to measure model quality, drift, and downstream correctness of code changes.</li> <li>Own hiring, performance development, 1:1s, and career growth for your reports; grow a high-performing, inclusive team.</li> <li>Be accountable for production readiness: on-call expectations, postmortems, SLIs/SLOs, and operational playbooks for codegen services.</li> <li>Maintain engineering rigor around data pipelines, model inputs, determinism, reproducibility, and reproducible CI/CD for model + infra changes.<br><br></li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Proven experience in software engineering and applied science, with a focus on engineering LLM-based systems in production</li> <li>Demonstrated experience managing small teams of software engineers and/or applied scientists, with a track record of delivering high-quality products</li> <li>Strong software development skills and proficiency in Python and Go</li> <li>Strong understanding of machine learning theory, statistics, and fundamentals</li> <li>Excellent communication abilities to convey complex technical concepts clearly</li> <li>A collaborative mindset and proven experience in working in cross-functional teams</li> <li>A proactive approach with a passion for continuous learning and innovation</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to use AI coding tools in day-to-day workflows and validate, critique, and refine AI-generated output</li> <li>You’re motivated to push the boundaries of how AI can improve software engineering best practices and contribute to building AI-enabled products</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.<br><br></em></p> <p><strong>Benefits &amp; Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing</li> <li>Intradepartmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, ability to join our Community Guilds (Datadog employee resource groups)</li> <li>Access to Inclusion Talks, our internal panel discussions</li> <li>Free, global mental health benefits for employees and dependents age 6+</li> <li>Competitive global benefits<br><br></li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>Datadog offers a competitive salary and equity package, and may include variable compensation. jobs.location: Boston, Massachusetts, USA; Denver, Colorado, USA; New York, New York, USA
jobs.description: <p>The Manager of Networking at Datadog leads the global management of network services across all Datadog offices worldwide. This role is responsible for ensuring seamless, high-performance Wi-Fi and direct internet access in our global offices and conference room technology, supporting a rapidly growing global enterprise. As Datadog continues to grow rapidly, this role plays a critical part in scaling both the team and network infrastructure to meet increasing demand. This is a hybrid role that sits in global headquarters in New York city and requires three days in the office each week with occasional travel to our offices around the world.<br><br></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead the global network engineering teams, managing both full-time employees and third-party vendors to ensure consistent and high-quality service delivery for Datadog offices worldwide.</li> <li>Oversee the design, deployment and scaling of office network infrastructure, including Wi-Fi (Cisco Meraki) and edge networking devices (Cisco, Palo Alto, Juniper, and others), ensuring these services operate according to Datadog's defined service level objectives.</li> <li>Define and implement standards, policies, and processes for network infrastructure to ensure security, reliability and scalability.</li> <li>Collaborate with IT Security, Enterprise Technology, and Workplace teams to align network services with broader IT and business objectives.</li> <li>Develop and track operational metrics for service availability, network performance, driving continuous improvement and optimization.<br><br></li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>An experienced people manager with at least 2+ years of leadership experience managing teams of network engineers.</li> <li>Proven expertise in Wi-Fi network engineering, including deep knowledge of Cisco Meraki and edge networking solutions from vendors like Cisco, Palo Alto and Juniper.</li> <li>Experience managing large-scale office technology projects in global enterprises with more than 10 offices and 7,000+ employees, ensuring infrastructure keeps pace with rapid company growth.</li> <li>Strong vendor management skills, with experience selecting, negotiating, and overseeing third-party providers for network services.</li> <li>Excellent communication and cross-functional collaboration skills, with the ability to work across IT, Workplace, jobs.title: Product Designer II, CCM
jobs.description: <p>We’re looking for a <strong>Product Designer 2</strong> to join the <strong>Cloud Cost Management (CCM)</strong> team at Datadog, focused specifically on <strong>commitment programs.</strong> In this role, you’ll design user experiences that help customers understand, choose, and manage cloud commitments—unlocking significant cost savings in a highly technical and high-impact product area.</p> <p>You’ll partner closely with product managers and engineers to shape workflows that span discovery, optimization, and long-term management of commitments. This is a hands-on role with ownership over meaningful features, where you’ll help define information architecture, interaction models, and visual direction as the product evolves from an AWS-only experience to a true multi-cloud offering (AWS, Azure, and GCP).</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em><em><br></em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead end-to-end design work for commitment programs, from early discovery and concept exploration through high-fidelity design and production.</li> <li>Own key workflows and features, helping evolve the product from an MVP into a differentiated, multi-cloud experience.</li> <li>Collaborate closely with cross-functional partners (PMs, engineers, and fellow designers) to define problems, explore tradeoffs, and ship high-quality solutions quickly.</li> <li>Help shape information architecture and interaction patterns as the product expands across different cloud providers with varying commitment models.</li> <li>Contribute to Datadog’s design system and ensure consistency across CCM and the broader Datadog platform.</li> <li>Advocate for user needs through thoughtful design decisions and clear communication with stakeholders at all levels.</li> <li>Develop deep expertise in commitment programs while sharing knowledge and best practices across the broader design team.</li> <li>Champion thoughtful, polished visual design that raises the bar for the product’s look and feel.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are</strong></p> <ul> <li>You have 3+ years of experience in digital product design, ideally within SaaS or technically complex domains.</li> <li>Your portfolio demonstrates strong end-to-end design execution with measurable impact and high-quality user experiences.</li> <li>You are an excellent communicator, able to clearly articulate design rationale and collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams.</li> <li>You’re proficient with modern design and prototyping tools (e.g., Figma, Sketch, Framer, InVision, Principle). Bonus if you have utilized tools like Cursor or Claude Code in your design workflow.</li> <li>You think in systems and are comfortable working within, and contributing to, component-based design patterns.</li> <li>You value feedback, iteration, and collaboration, and you actively help raise the quality of work across the team.</li> <li>You bring creativity and visual craft to your work.<br><br></li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing</li> <li>Intradepartmental mentor and buddy programs for in-house networking</li> <li>Inclusive company culture with access to Community Guilds and Inclusion Talks</li> <li>Free, global mental health benefits for employees and dependents (age 6+)</li> <li>Competitive global benefits package</li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about #DatadogLife on <a href="https://www.instagram.com/datadoghq/?hl=en" target="_blank">Instagram</a>, <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/company/datadog" target="_blank">LinkedIn,</a> and <a href="https://learn.datadoghq.com/" target="_blank">Datadog Learning Center. </a></p> <hr> <p data-renderer-start-pos="215"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Equal Opportunity at Datadog:</strong></p> <p>Datadog is proud to offer <a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf" target="_blank">equal employment opportunity</a> to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and other characteristics protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Here are our <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/candidate-legal-notices/" target="_blank">Candidate Legal Notices</a> for your reference.&nbsp;</p> <p>Datadog endeavors to make our Careers Page accessible to all users. If you would like to contact us regarding the accessibility of our website or need assistance completing the application process, please complete <a href="https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeoJeduEZ2NdZF_65uZmWUrCM-zee7yADXX1s6Xr9zAGq8iKA/viewform" target="_blank">this form</a>. jobs.title: Technical Escalations Engineer 3 - Synthetics (Mexico City)
jobs.description: <p>At Datadog, we don't just support our products, we master them. As Datadog's in-house product experts, the Technical Escalation Engineering (TEE) team plays a critical role in driving our global success. We enable our customers, from the world's most innovative startups to the largest enterprises, to harness the full power of Datadog's platform, ensuring their growth, reliability, and performance.</p> <p>Through deep technical expertise, relentless problem-solving, and exceptional customer engagement, we educate, guide, and troubleshoot, delivering high-impact solutions that shape the customer experience. Whether through hands-on technical call, in-depth fact findings meeting, or complex investigations, we set the gold standard for technical excellence and customer advocacy.</p> <p>As part of our TEE team, you'll tackle the most challenging technical problems, collaborate directly with Engineering and Product to refine and evolve our platform, and mentor teams worldwide, elevating the technical bar at every level.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Develop deep technical expertise, continuously learn as the product evolves, and become the go-to authority in your domain.</li> <li>Investigate complex escalations, lead high-stakes technical calls, and drive solutions for our most critical customer challenges with urgency and precision.</li> <li>Run engaging office hours, deliver impactful learning sessions, and mentor the Global Support Engineering team (GSE), ensuring they’re equipped to handle any challenge.</li> <li>Partner with Engineering and Product to proactively identify gaps, drive meaningful improvements, and advocate for customers in shaping the evolution of our platform.</li> <li>Become the Synthetics expert within the Technical Solutions team, supporting deep technical investigations around API and browser test behavior.</li> <li>Lead escalations involving failed tests, flakiness, unexpected downtime alerts, test scheduling issues, and false positives or negatives.</li> <li>Collaborate with Product and Engineering to escalate bugs, propose test engine improvements, and refine the customer experience.</li> <li>Create reusable troubleshooting guides, internal tools, and scalable workflows to help TSEs diagnose and resolve synthetic test issues quickly.</li> <li>Enable the global Solutions team through live sessions, async learning, and technical mentoring on how to confidently support Synthetic Monitoring.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>A self-driven problem solver with an obsession for excellence and continuous learning. You thrive in ambiguity, take initiative, and push beyond expectations.</li> <li>A strategic thinker who defaults to a client-centric approach, proactively identifying opportunities to enhance customer experience while balancing technical feasibility.</li> <li>An independent, fast learner, capable of picking up new technologies, adapting to evolving priorities, and crafting innovative solutions to complex technical issues.</li> <li>A customer advocate, experienced in high-stakes technical support or solutions engineering, comfortable tackling difficult customer challenges, and turning problems into opportunities.</li> <li>A collaborator and mentor, who enjoys sharing knowledge, raising the technical bar for the team, and contributing to a culture of excellence.</li> <li>Experienced in proactive monitoring and understand the value of simulating user behavior to catch issues before they impact real customers.</li> <li>Comfortable troubleshooting API and browser tests, including multi-step workflows, variable substitution, assertions, global variables, and test retries.</li> <li>Familiar with front-end behavior, page load performance, HTTP protocols, headers, and network errors,&nbsp; and how they relate to test failures.</li> <li>Confident working with Synthetic CI/CD integrations, private locations, and the interplay between synthetic signals and backend performance data.</li> <li>Detail-oriented and customer-focused, with a passion for reducing noise, increasing test reliability, and improving detection accuracy.</li> </ul> <h3>&nbsp;</h3> <p><strong>Bonus Points</strong></p> <ul> <li>Computer Science or Engineering majors</li> <li>Experienced in using Zendesk, Jira, Confluence, or similar softwares.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Generous and competitive<a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/global-benefits/"> global</a> benefits</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing</li> <li>Intradepartmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, ability to join our Community Guilds (Datadog employee resource groups)</li> <li>Best in breed onboarding</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about #DatadogLife on <a href="https://www.instagram.com/datadoghq/?hl=en" target="_blank">Instagram</a>, <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/company/datadog" target="_blank">LinkedIn,</a> and <a href="https://learn.datadoghq.com/" target="_blank">Datadog Learning Center. </a></p> <hr> <p data-renderer-start-pos="215"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Equal Opportunity at Datadog:</strong></p> <p>Datadog is proud to offer <a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf" target="_blank">equal employment opportunity</a> to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and other characteristics protected by law. Here are our <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/candidate-legal-notices/" target="_blank">Candidate Legal Notices</a> for your reference.&nbsp;</p> <p>Datadog endeavors to make our Careers Page accessible to all users.
