jobs.title: Area Manager
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>About Flexport:&nbsp;</strong></h2> <p>At Flexport, we believe global trade can move the human race forward. That’s why it’s our mission to make global commerce so easy there will be more of it. We’re shaping the future of a $10T industry with solutions powered by innovative technology and exceptional people. Today, companies of all sizes—from emerging brands to Fortune 500s—use Flexport technology to move more than $19B of merchandise across 112 countries a year.&nbsp;</p> <p>The recent global supply chain crisis has put Flexport center stage as we continue to play a pivotal role in how goods move around the world. We are proud to have the support of the best investors in the game who believe in our mission, solutions and people. Ready to tackle global challenges that impact business, society, and the environment? Come join us.</p></div><h2>The Opportunity:</h2> <p>We are currently seeking individuals for a full-time position that involves launching and operating one of our inaugural fulfillment centers. As a key member of the Flexport team, you will take on the role of a people leader, responsible for managing a process and a team on the operating floor. This position offers a unique opportunity to invest in others and cultivate a collaborative leadership style. By utilizing the tools, processes, and operations that drive Flexport's mission to make global trade easy for everyone, you will contribute to shaping the future of logistics. We are looking for high-potential candidates who are eager to take on this challenge and be part of our dynamic and innovative organization. Join us in our mission to revolutionize global trade and make a lasting impact on the industry.</p> <p>This role is onsite in Atlanta, GA.&nbsp;</p> <h2>You will:</h2> <ul> <li>Coach, manage, and develop a team of 30-100.</li> <li>Communicate policies to your team and act as the primary information source for the team, maintaining compliance, consistency, and taking corrective action when needed.</li> <li>Create, manage, and support recognition programs across your department.</li> <li>Support all safety programs and OSHA compliance to ensure a safe work environment for all associates.</li> <li>Ensure procedures are followed for building security and product loss prevention.</li> <li>Be an integral part of our Operations team as an Area Manager of one of our largest fulfillment partners located across the country.</li> <li>Own performance reporting for your areas of responsibility within the FC, including, but not limited to, on-time receiving and shipping, inventory accuracy, and packing quality.</li> <li>Consistently hold yourself and your team to strict SLA expectations, providing clear and concise verbal and written reports of performance and action plans when needed.</li> <li>Facilitate troubleshooting with relevant cross-functional parties for fulfillment-related problems.</li> <li>Serve as an escalation point to mitigate risks and resolve issues.</li> <li>Measure and respond to gaps in inventory accuracy, facilitating strategic initiatives to identify root causes and resolve them.</li> <li>Review defect rates to identify trends and expose opportunities to improve operations both internally and across the network.</li> <li>Track learnings and best practices to contribute to Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).</li> <li>Help prioritize the resolution of systemic problems, working closely with our Growth and Engineering teams.</li> </ul> <h2>You should have:</h2> <ul> <li>2+ years managing staff of 5 or more.</li> <li>Must be able to lift up to 49 pounds with or without reasonable accommodation.</li> <li>Must be willing and able to frequently push, pull, squat, bend, and reach.</li> <li>Must be able to stand/walk for up to 10-12 hours.</li> <li>Must be able to work in an environment where the noise level varies and can be loud.</li> <li>Must be able to work in an environment where the temperature may vary between 60 and 90 degrees and will occasionally exceed 90 degrees.</li> <li>Must be able to work on a secure mezzanine at a height of up to 40 feet (applies to buildings with mezzanines). This position requires a candidate with the proven ability to drive process improvements and the ability to keep pace with our growth while motivating others to meet challenges in a deadline-driven environment.</li> <li>Willing and able to work any permanent shift that may include overnights and holidays. Note that there is a high chance that your shift may overlap into part of the weekend.</li> <li>Demonstrated collaboration with internal and external business partners to deliver greater operational performance, efficiency, and quality.</li> <li>Precise communication around external partner decisions, issues, risks, and new processes.</li> <li>Keen ability to distinguish internal versus external problems causes to properly focus the correct resources to resolve issues quickly.</li> <li>Exceptional prioritization skills to ensure timely resolutions that are appropriate for the severity and impact of the situation.</li> <li>Experience leveraging problem-solving and data analysis skills to drive high judgment decisions.</li> <li>Strong presentation and interpersonal skills.</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to work in a fast-paced, ambiguous, deadline-oriented work environment.</li> <li>Ability to adapt in a rapid growth, startup environment.</li> <li>Strong problem-solving skills.</li> <li>Great attention to detail + appreciation for the complexity associated with the consistent achievement of operational tasks.</li> <li>Personable and collaborative; strong affinity for working cross-functionally.</li> <li>Strong computer literacy skills, including usage of Google Sheets/Excel.</li> <li>Incredibly hardworking and willing to do what it takes for us to reach our goals.</li> <li>Experience in warehousing, fulfillment, or transportation/logistics management.</li> <li>Experience working with SQL, creating and running reports.</li> </ul> <p>#LI-Onsite</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><div class="c-message_kit__gutter"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter__right" data-qa="message_content"> <div class="c-message_kit__blocks c-message_kit__blocks--rich_text"> <div class="c-message__message_blocks c-message__message_blocks--rich_text" data-qa="message-text"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer" data-qa="block-kit-renderer"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper--first"> <div class="p-rich_text_block"> <div class="p-rich_text_section"> <div><span style="font-family: arial, sans-serif;"><em>The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position in the posting's respective region. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range displayed, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education and / or training.&nbsp;</em></span></div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div></div><div class="title">The US base salary range for this position (this does not include bonus, equity and benefits):</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$64,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$81,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><div id="1704749847.557549" class="c-virtual_list__item" data-qa="virtual-list-item" data-item-key="1704749847.557549"> <div class="c-message_kit__background p-message_pane_message__message c-message_kit__message" data-qa="message_container" data-qa-unprocessed="false" data-qa-placeholder="false"> <div class="c-message_kit__hover" data-qa-hover="true"> <div class="c-message_kit__actions c-message_kit__actions--default"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter__right" data-qa="message_content"> <div class="c-message_kit__blocks c-message_kit__blocks--rich_text"> <div class="c-message__message_blocks c-message__message_blocks--rich_text" data-qa="message-text"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer" data-qa="block-kit-renderer"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper--first"> <div class="p-rich_text_block"> <h4>Commitment to Equal Opportunity</h4> <p>At Flexport, our ability to fulfill our mission of making global commerce easy and accessible relies on having a diverse, dedicated and engaged workforce. jobs.title: Area Manager- New Grad
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>About Flexport:&nbsp;</strong></h2> <p>At Flexport, we believe global trade can move the human race forward. That’s why it’s our mission to make global commerce so easy there will be more of it. We’re shaping the future of a $10T industry with solutions powered by innovative technology and exceptional people. Today, companies of all sizes—from emerging brands to Fortune 500s—use Flexport technology to move more than $19B of merchandise across 112 countries a year.&nbsp;</p> <p>The recent global supply chain crisis has put Flexport center stage as we continue to play a pivotal role in how goods move around the world. We are proud to have the support of the best investors in the game who believe in our mission, solutions and people. Ready to tackle global challenges that impact business, society, and the environment? Come join us.</p></div><h2>The Opportunity:</h2> <p>We are currently seeking individuals for a full-time position that involves launching and operating one of our inaugural fulfillment centers. As a key member of the Flexport team, you will take on the role of a people leader, responsible for managing a process and a team on the operating floor. This position offers a unique opportunity to invest in others and cultivate a collaborative leadership style. By utilizing the tools, processes, and operations that drive Flexport's mission to make global trade easy for everyone, you will contribute to shaping the future of logistics. We are looking for high-potential candidates who are eager to take on this challenge and be part of our dynamic and innovative organization. Join us in our mission to revolutionize global trade and make a lasting impact on the industry.</p> <p>This role is onsite in Atlanta, GA.&nbsp;</p> <h2>You will:</h2> <ul> <li>Coach, manage, and develop a team of 30-100.</li> <li>Communicate policies to your team and act as the primary information source for the team, maintaining compliance, consistency, and taking corrective action when needed.</li> <li>Create, manage, and support recognition programs across your department.</li> <li>Support all safety programs and OSHA compliance to ensure a safe work environment for all associates.</li> <li>Ensure procedures are followed for building security and product loss prevention.</li> <li>Be an integral part of our Operations team as an Area Manager of one of our largest fulfillment partners located across the country.</li> <li>Own performance reporting for your areas of responsibility within the FC, including, but not limited to, on-time receiving and shipping, inventory accuracy, and packing quality.</li> <li>Consistently hold yourself and your team to strict SLA expectations, providing clear and concise verbal and written reports of performance and action plans when needed.</li> <li>Facilitate troubleshooting with relevant cross-functional parties for fulfillment-related problems.</li> <li>Serve as an escalation point to mitigate risks and resolve issues.</li> <li>Measure and respond to gaps in inventory accuracy, facilitating strategic initiatives to identify root causes and resolve them.</li> <li>Review defect rates to identify trends and expose opportunities to improve operations both internally and across the network.</li> <li>Track learnings and best practices to contribute to Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).</li> <li>Help prioritize the resolution of systemic problems, working closely with our Growth and Engineering teams.</li> </ul> <h2>You should have:</h2> <ul> <li>2+ years managing staff of 5 or more.</li> <li>Must be able to lift up to 49 pounds with or without reasonable accommodation.</li> <li>Must be willing and able to frequently push, pull, squat, bend, and reach.</li> <li>Must be able to stand/walk for up to 10-12 hours.</li> <li>Must be able to work in an environment where the noise level varies and can be loud.</li> <li>Must be able to work in an environment where the temperature may vary between 60 and 90 degrees and will occasionally exceed 90 degrees.</li> <li>Must be able to work on a secure mezzanine at a height of up to 40 feet (applies to buildings with mezzanines). This position requires a candidate with the proven ability to drive process improvements and the ability to keep pace with our growth while motivating others to meet challenges in a deadline-driven environment.</li> <li>Willing and able to work any permanent shift that may include overnights and holidays. Note that there is a high chance that your shift may overlap into part of the weekend.</li> <li>Demonstrated collaboration with internal and external business partners to deliver greater operational performance, efficiency, and quality.</li> <li>Precise communication around external partner decisions, issues, risks, and new processes.</li> <li>Keen ability to distinguish internal versus external problems causes to properly focus the correct resources to resolve issues quickly.</li> <li>Exceptional prioritization skills to ensure timely resolutions that are appropriate for the severity and impact of the situation.</li> <li>Experience leveraging problem-solving and data analysis skills to drive high judgment decisions.</li> <li>Strong presentation and interpersonal skills.</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to work in a fast-paced, ambiguous, deadline-oriented work environment.</li> <li>Ability to adapt in a rapid growth, startup environment.</li> <li>Strong problem-solving skills.</li> <li>Great attention to detail + appreciation for the complexity associated with the consistent achievement of operational tasks.</li> <li>Personable and collaborative; strong affinity for working cross-functionally.</li> <li>Strong computer literacy skills, including usage of Google Sheets/Excel.</li> <li>Incredibly hardworking and willing to do what it takes for us to reach our goals.</li> <li>Experience in warehousing, fulfillment, or transportation/logistics management.</li> <li>Experience working with SQL, creating and running reports.</li> </ul> <p>#LI-Onsite</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><div class="c-message_kit__gutter"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter__right" data-qa="message_content"> <div class="c-message_kit__blocks c-message_kit__blocks--rich_text"> <div class="c-message__message_blocks c-message__message_blocks--rich_text" data-qa="message-text"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer" data-qa="block-kit-renderer"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper--first"> <div class="p-rich_text_block"> <div class="p-rich_text_section"> <div><span style="font-family: arial, sans-serif;"><em>The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position in the posting's respective region. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range displayed, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education and / or training.&nbsp;</em></span></div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div></div><div class="title">The US base salary range for this position (this does not include bonus, equity and benefits):</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$64,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$81,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><div id="1704749847.557549" class="c-virtual_list__item" data-qa="virtual-list-item" data-item-key="1704749847.557549"> <div class="c-message_kit__background p-message_pane_message__message c-message_kit__message" data-qa="message_container" data-qa-unprocessed="false" data-qa-placeholder="false"> <div class="c-message_kit__hover" data-qa-hover="true"> <div class="c-message_kit__actions c-message_kit__actions--default"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter__right" data-qa="message_content"> <div class="c-message_kit__blocks c-message_kit__blocks--rich_text"> <div class="c-message__message_blocks c-message__message_blocks--rich_text" data-qa="message-text"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer" data-qa="block-kit-renderer"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper--first"> <div class="p-rich_text_block"> <h4>Commitment to Equal Opportunity</h4> <p>At Flexport, our ability to fulfill our mission of making global commerce easy and accessible relies on having a diverse, dedicated and engaged workforce. jobs.title: Software Engineer I
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>About Flexport:&nbsp;</strong></h2> <p>At Flexport, we believe global trade can move the human race forward. That’s why it’s our mission to make global commerce so easy there will be more of it. We’re shaping the future of a $10T industry with solutions powered by innovative technology and exceptional people. Today, companies of all sizes—from emerging brands to Fortune 500s—use Flexport technology to move more than $19B of merchandise across 112 countries a year.&nbsp;</p> <p>The recent global supply chain crisis has put Flexport center stage as we continue to play a pivotal role in how goods move around the world. We are proud to have the support of the best investors in the game who believe in our mission, solutions and people. Ready to tackle global challenges that impact business, society, and the environment? Come join us.</p></div><p><strong>The Opportunity</strong></p> <p>This is a unique opportunity to join a new engineering team at the ground floor. As an SDE 1, you will learn from senior and staff level engineers while contributing to real production systems that power global trade. We are building Flexport’s client-facing rates platform and a new self-serve booking experience, moving the company from an assisted-sales model to a tech-run one.</p> <p>You will help build the "front door" of our Client App: the interfaces and services that allow customers to see pricing and book shipments without manual intervention. This is an <strong>AI-first team</strong> where you will see firsthand how intelligent automation is applied to real-world logistics. If you are looking for a high-growth environment where you can see your code impact thousands of shipments across 112 countries, this is the team for you.</p> <h3><strong>You Will</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Design, develop, test, deploy, and maintain systems that represent the real-world intricacies of global logistics and pricing logic.</li> <li>Work within an AI-integrated codebase, learning how to build and maintain services that support autonomous booking and intelligent quoting.</li> <li>Contribute to code reviews to maintain code quality, share knowledge, and learn from experienced mentors on the team.</li> <li>Collaborate with engineering, product, and design to translate complex business problems into elegant technical solutions.</li> <li>Build highly available, secure, and distributed services within a modern microservices architecture.</li> <li>Build and launch products rapidly and incrementally: we ship to production dozens of times per day.</li> <li>Ensure high-quality standards by incorporating metrics and monitoring into every service you touch.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>You Should Have</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.</li> <li>1+ years of professional experience in software development with demonstrated coding skills: <strong>strong internship experience in a product-focused environment is highly valued.</strong></li> <li>Proficiency in modern programming languages: specifically Java, Ruby, or TypeScript.</li> <li>Familiarity with technologies like databases, API development, and modern frontend frameworks like React.</li> <li><strong>AI Curiosity:</strong> A strong interest in how AI and LLMs are transforming software development and a desire to learn how to build with these technologies.</li> <li><strong>A self-starter attitude:</strong> You are curious, eager to learn, and when you see a problem, you look for a way to solve it.</li> <li><strong>Product Curiosity:</strong> An interest in how users interact with software and a desire to understand the "why" behind the features you build.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>It’s a Plus If You Have</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Experience with elements of our tech stack: Java, Spring Boot, Ruby on Rails, React, AWS.</li> <li>Experience using AI coding assistants to accelerate your development workflow.</li> <li>Ability to convey technical ideas in common terms to business stakeholders and product managers.</li> <li>Excellent written and verbal communication skills.</li> </ul> <p>#LI-Onsite</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><div class="c-message_kit__gutter"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter__right" jobs.title: Software Engineer II
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>About Flexport:&nbsp;</strong></h2> <p>At Flexport, we believe global trade can move the human race forward. That’s why it’s our mission to make global commerce so easy there will be more of it. We’re shaping the future of a $10T industry with solutions powered by innovative technology and exceptional people. Today, companies of all sizes—from emerging brands to Fortune 500s—use Flexport technology to move more than $19B of merchandise across 112 countries a year.&nbsp;</p> <p>The recent global supply chain crisis has put Flexport center stage as we continue to play a pivotal role in how goods move around the world. We are proud to have the support of the best investors in the game who believe in our mission, solutions and people. Ready to tackle global challenges that impact business, society, and the environment? Come join us.</p></div><h3><strong>The Opportunity</strong></h3> <p>Join a new engineering team in San Francisco building Flexport’s client-facing rates platform and self-serve freight booking experience from the ground up. You will work alongside experienced engineers and have direct ownership of features that move the company from an assisted-sales model to a tech-run one for the long tail of our client base.</p> <p>This team owns two of the highest-leverage surfaces in the Client App: how clients see pricing and how they book without manual intervention. Rates sit at the center of every freight decision, and you will build the systems that power rate visibility, quoting, and pricing intelligence across ocean, air, and trucking. You will also help build the infrastructure that integrates AI-powered automation into the booking flow. This is a rare opportunity to build the "front door" of an $8.6 trillion industry, turning the complexity of global trade into a seamless digital experience for thousands of shippers.</p> <h3><strong>You Will</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Design, develop, test, deploy, and maintain systems within a microservices architecture that power rate visibility and autonomous booking.</li> <li>Own the entire lifecycle of a feature: from database schema design and scalable backend APIs to building the React-based frontend applications our customers use.</li> <li>Support the integration of AI models and automated workflows to make the pricing and booking experience increasingly intelligent.</li> <li>Collaborate with engineering, product, and design to translate complex business problems into elegant, production-ready technical solutions.</li> <li>Contribute to code reviews to maintain high quality standards, share knowledge, and mentor peer engineers.</li> <li>Build highly available, secure, and distributed systems that can scale with Flexport’s global volume.</li> <li>Rapidly build and launch products incrementally, leveraging best practices around CI/CD and shipping to production dozens of times per day.</li> <li>Ensure high-quality standards by incorporating metrics, monitoring, and applying learnings from post-mortems to all services.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>You Should Have</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.</li> <li>3+ years of professional software engineering experience with a proven track record of shipping production-grade applications.</li> <li><strong>Full-Stack Capability:</strong> You are comfortable owning a feature from end-to-end: working with databases, backend services (Java/Ruby), and frontend frameworks (React).</li> <li><strong>Technical Rigor:</strong> A passion for simple, well-tested code that is easy to extend and hard to break.</li> <li><strong>AI Familiarity:</strong> Experience or interest in working with AI APIs and building software that leverages machine learning models to automate user tasks.</li> <li>2+ years of design and architecture knowledge as well as familiarity with object-oriented analysis and design patterns.</li> <li>Proficiency with modern programming languages: specifically Java, Ruby, or TypeScript.</li> <li>Proficiency with deploying, monitoring, and maintaining production services on cloud infrastructure (AWS).</li> </ul> <h3><strong>It’s a Plus If You Have</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Experience with elements of our tech stack: Java, Spring Boot, Ruby on Rails, React, AWS.</li> <li>Experience in fintech, pricing engines, or booking systems.</li> <li>Experience in logistics, supply chain, or freight technology.</li> <li><strong>A bias for action:</strong> When you see a problem, you fix it.</li> <li>Excellent written and verbal communication skills: you can convey technical ideas in common terms to business stakeholders and product managers.</li> </ul> <p>#LI-onsite</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><div jobs.title: Product Growth Partner
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>From Fivetran’s founding until now, our mission has remained the same: to make access to data as simple and reliable as electricity. With Fivetran, customer data arrives in their warehouses, canonical and ready to query, with no engineering or maintenance required. We’re proud that more organizations continue to leverage our technology every day to become truly data-driven.</p></div><p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong><strong>About the Role</strong></strong></span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Fivetran is the one platform for moving the world’s data from every source to every destination to power every innovation.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">To support our growth, our Product Growth Partners (PGPs) are central to Fivetran’s self-service motion, enabling thousands of customers to succeed at scale. PGPs design and deliver many-to-1 enablement programs, proactively reduce friction for self-service users, and intervene with precision when accounts need additional guidance. PGPs ensure that customers who can self-serve do so seamlessly, while those who need help receive the right support at the right time.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">This is a full-time, hybrid position based out of our Denver, CO office. Our hybrid work model offers a blend of remote flexibility and in-person collaboration, including two days in the office each week to connect and build as a team.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>What You'll Do</strong></span></p> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong><strong>Accelerate Self-Service Success</strong></strong></span> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Guide new trial users and existing customers to value through scalable programs such as webinars, office hours, and educational sessions.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Direct customers/prospects to resources (help docs, videos, automations) that resolve blockers without requiring 1:1 intervention.</span></li> </ul> </li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong><strong>Deliver Targeted Customer Interventions</strong></strong></span> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Identify accounts showing clear setup friction or usage blockers and step in with qualified, high-value interventions.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Drive trial-to-paid conversions where PGP guidance removes obstacles and enables customers to commit to a paid plan.</span></li> </ul> </li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong><strong>Run Many-to-1 Programs</strong></strong></span> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Design, host, and optimize scalable programs (hands-on labs, “best practice” webinars, AMA sessions) that drive activation, adoption, and expansion at scale.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Measure program reach, engagement quality, and downstream business impact (trial conversion, connector expansion, MAR growth).</span></li> </ul> </li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong><strong>Enable Expansion Readiness</strong></strong></span> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Identify accounts with expansion potential (new connectors, workloads, or use cases) and surface them for sales or CSM follow-up when appropriate.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Share preventative guidance to reduce drop-offs and ensure smooth adoption as accounts scale.</span></li> </ul> </li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong><strong>Advocate for Customers</strong></strong></span> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Capture customer insights and funnel them back to product, engineering, and marketing teams to improve self-service experiences.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Build scalable resources (guides, templates, content) that multiply across the customer base.</span></li> </ul> </li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong><strong>Handle Reactive Needs</strong></strong></span> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Support “lights-on” requests (low-touch commercial tasks, small contract adjustments, billing/account escalations) efficiently and effectively.</span><br><span style="font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</span></li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Skills We're Looking For</strong></span></p> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Experience: 1–2 years in customer success, product support, enablement, or similar role.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Customer-Centric Mindset: Ability to diagnose customer friction points and provide clear, scalable solutions.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Technical Proficiency: Foundational knowledge in SQL, APIs, cloud data warehouses (Snowflake, Databricks, BigQuery, Redshift), and basic data ELT concepts.&nbsp;</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Program Builder: Experience running group enablement (webinars, labs, training, or community events) preferred.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Analytical Skills: Comfortable using CRM and product analytics to identify usage patterns, triggers, and opportunities.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Collaboration: Proven ability to partner with cross-functional teams (product, sales, support, RevOps) to improve customer outcomes.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Communication: Strong written and verbal communication skills for both 1:1 and many-to-1 interactions.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Adaptability: Thrives in an environment where success is measured by customer outcomes and scalable impact, not quota attainment</span><span style="font-size: 10pt;">.</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">#LI-Hybrid #LI-LW1</span></p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>The compensation range displayed on this job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire compensation for the target position and level, and may include sales incentives or target bonuses depending on the role. &nbsp;Our compensation ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Our job titles may span more than one career level. Within the range, individual compensation is determined by additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, relevant education or training, business need, market demands. The compensation range is subject to change and may be modified in the future. Your recruiter can share more about the specific compensation range for your location during the hiring process.</p></div><div class="title">Denver Pay Range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$94,496</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$113,400 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p>&nbsp;</p> <p class="p-rich_text_section" style="line-height: 1.3;"><strong data-stringify-type="bold">Perks and Benefits</strong></p> <ul> <li>100% employer-paid medical insurance<strong>*</strong></li> <li>Generous paid time-off policy (PTO), plus paid sick time, inclusive parental leave policy, holidays, and volunteer days off</li> <li>RSU stock grants*</li> <li>Professional development and training opportunities</li> <li>Company virtual happy hours, free food, and fun team-building activities</li> <li>Monthly cell phone stipend</li> <li>Access to an innovative mental health support platform that offers personalized care and resources in areas such as: therapy, coaching, and self-guided mindfulness exercises for all covered employees and their covered dependents.</li> </ul> <p style="line-height: 1.3;"><em><strong>*</strong>May vary by country and worker type - please reach out to your recruiter for more information</em></p> <p style="line-height: 1.3;"><em>Click <a href="https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1CdawUwjo1Q1B7ghZJ_owdmmHB8VtqhBu?usp=sharing" target="_blank">here</a> to learn more about Fivetran's Benefits by Region.</em></p> <hr> <p style="line-height: 1.3;">We’re honored to be <a class="c-link" href="https://fivetran.com/blog/hvr-acquisition-series-d" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://fivetran.com/blog/hvr-acquisition-series-d" data-sk="tooltip_parent">valued at over $5.6 billion</a>, but more importantly, we’re proud of our&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="https://fivetran.com/culture" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://fivetran.com/culture" data-sk="tooltip_parent">core values of Get Stuck In, Do the Right Thing, and One Team, One Dream</a>. jobs.title: Customer Success Associate
jobs.description: <p>As a Customer Success Associate, you will&nbsp; focus on the long term strategic relationship with our customers, drive adoption, and identify growth opportunities across a high volume of Datadog customers to ensure successful product use and retention. You’ll be an advocate for the customer internally and focus on providing a positive experience for a high volume of accounts. Associates follow a well-defined methodology to identify the customer’s unique needs and clearly convey the value of the Datadog product. As we scale our Customer Success team, furthering personal development and team success is a top priority.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Identify target opportunities, manage the full sales cycle and proactively build strategic relationships with customers and focus on retention.&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li>Manage requests from your book of business such as but not limited to technical support escalations, billing questions, documentation/enablement requests to ensure a positive customer experience</li> <li>Pivot account management strategy based on different customer requirements and needs seamlessly and efficiently</li> <li>Act as an advisor to customers to ensure they’re leveraging the solution effectively</li> <li>Monitor and identify usage trends to uncover renewal risks and support greater adoption rates</li> <li>Collaborate cross-functionally with internal Datadog teams (support, product, finance, and legal)</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Customer-minded, always trying to provide the most value possible</li> <li>Driven and motivated by a career in sales with a strong desire to grow a career in Customer Success.</li> <li>A strong communicator (written and verbal) with excellent attention to detail</li> <li>Experienced in independently negotiating contract terms and overcoming pricing objections for up-sell/cross sell activities</li> <li>0-2 years of experience in a customer account facing role and desire to grow a career in Customer Success</li> <li>Passionate about building long term and lasting relationships</li> </ul> <p style="color: white;">&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Best-in-breed onboarding</li> <li>Sales training in MEDDIC and Command of the Message&nbsp;</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, opportunity to join our Community Guilds</li> <li>Intra-departmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking&nbsp;</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing&nbsp;</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSU) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Generous global benefits</li> </ul> <p style="color: white;">&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about #DatadogLife on <a href="https://www.instagram.com/datadoghq/?hl=en" target="_blank">Instagram</a>, <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/company/datadog" target="_blank">LinkedIn,</a> and <a href="https://learn.datadoghq.com/" target="_blank">Datadog Learning Center. </a></p> <hr> <p data-renderer-start-pos="215"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Equal Opportunity at Datadog:</strong></p> <p>Datadog is proud to offer <a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf" target="_blank">equal employment opportunity</a> to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and other characteristics protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Here are our <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/candidate-legal-notices/" target="_blank">Candidate Legal Notices</a> for your reference.&nbsp;</p> <p>Datadog endeavors to make our Careers Page accessible to all users. If you would like to contact us regarding the accessibility of our website or need assistance completing the application process, please complete <a href="https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeoJeduEZ2NdZF_65uZmWUrCM-zee7yADXX1s6Xr9zAGq8iKA/viewform" target="_blank">this form</a>. This form is for accommodation requests only and cannot be used to inquire about the status of applications.&nbsp;</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Privacy and AI Guidelines:</strong></p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662">Any information you submit to Datadog as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Datadog’s&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" data-sk="tooltip_parent">Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice</a>. For information on our AI policy, please visit <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/interviewing-at-datadog-ai-guidelines/" target="_blank">Interviewing at Datadog AI Guidelines</a>.</p></div>

We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Identify target opportunities, manage the full sales cycle and proactively build strategic relationships with customers and focus on retention.&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li>Manage requests from your book of business such as but not limited to technical support escalations, billing questions, documentation/enablement requests to ensure a positive customer experience</li> <li>Pivot account management strategy based on different customer requirements and needs seamlessly and efficiently</li> <li>Act as an advisor to customers to ensure they’re leveraging the solution effectively</li> <li>Monitor and identify usage trends to uncover renewal risks and support greater adoption rates</li> <li>Collaborate cross-functionally with internal Datadog teams (support, product, finance, and legal)</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Customer-minded, always trying to provide the most value possible</li> <li>Driven and motivated by a career in sales with a strong desire to grow a career in Customer Success.</li> <li>A strong communicator (written and verbal) with excellent attention to detail</li> <li>Experienced in independently negotiating contract terms and overcoming pricing objections for up-sell/cross sell activities</li> <li>2-5 years of experience in a customer account facing role and desire to grow a career in Customer Success</li> <li>Passionate about building long term and lasting relationships</li> <li>Proficiency in Vietnamese and/or Thai will be a plus</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Best-in-breed onboarding</li> <li>Sales training in MEDDIC and Command of the Message&nbsp;</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, opportunity to join our Community Guilds</li> <li>Intra-departmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking&nbsp;</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing&nbsp;</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSU) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Generous global benefits</li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. jobs.title: Datadog for Startups Engineering Lead
jobs.description: <p>The Datadog for Startups (DDFS) program helps the next generation of fast-scaling companies adopt best-in-class observability and security from day one. As the DDFS Engineering Lead, you’ll combine technical sales engineering expertise with ecosystem growth leadership to attract top startups into the program, showcase Datadog’s value early, and position Datadog as an essential platform for scaling.</p> <p>This role is ideal for someone who thrives at the intersection of startups, business development, technical evangelism, and software engineering—comfortable running a discovery call with a CTO one day, hosting a founder networking event the next, and even helping startups get Datadog instrumented quickly.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <p><strong>1. Technical Sales Engineering &amp; Forward-Deployed Engineering (FDE) project management</strong></p> <ul> <li> <p>Validate startup technical needs and current tooling during pre-program discovery.</p> </li> <li> <p>Deliver tailored demos that highlight Datadog’s differentiators, quick wins, and startup relevance (AI, infra scaling, security).</p> </li> <li> <p>Answer competitive engineering questions and position Datadog effectively against alternatives.</p> </li> <li> <p>Create urgency for adoption so startups realize value quickly within their credits period.</p> </li> <li> <p>Ensure a smooth technical onboarding experience by removing friction and showcasing “aha” moments.</p> </li> <li> <p>Help manage FDE engineering projects that are embedded directly with client sites, deeply understanding workflows, pain points, and business processes.</p> </li> <li> <p>Implement Datadog for clients, troubleshoot issues or connect clients to those in Datadog who can support.</p> </li> <li> <p>Translate technical details for business stakeholders and communicate customer needs back to product/engineering teams.</p> </li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong><strong>2. Ecosystem Growth &amp; Awareness</strong></strong></p> <ul> <li> <p>Represent Datadog for Startups at key events (accelerator demo days, hackathons, conferences).</p> </li> <li> <p>Host or co-host startup-focused webinars, dinners, workshops, and founder roundtables.</p> </li> <li> <p>Develop messaging, collateral, and thought leadership for founders and technical teams.</p> </li> <li> <p>Drive inbound interest and applications through community-building efforts.</p> </li> <li> <p>Work with the SF DDFS Lead to build and manage relationships with accelerators, VCs, and other ecosystem partners.</p> </li> <li> <p>Host office hours with select referral partner portfolio companies to actively communicate DDFS perks to their portfolios.</p> </li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong><strong>3. DDFS Program Improvements</strong></strong></p> <ul> <li> <p>Help the DDFS team automate processes across the full lifecycle of startup sourcing to conversion across internal tools such as Salesforce, Marketo, etc.</p> </li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong><strong>Who You Are:</strong></strong></p> <p><strong><strong>Must-Haves</strong></strong></p> <ul> <li> <p>5–7 years in sales engineering, solutions architecture, technical BD, forward-deployed engineer, software engineering at a SaaS/cloud company.</p> </li> <li> <p>Strong understanding of modern startup tech stacks, including cloud infrastructure, application frameworks, data platforms, and developer tooling</p> </li> <li> <p>Proven experience engaging with startups, founders, VCs, technical decision-makers and audiences.</p> </li> <li> <p>Excellent communication skills: equally effective in technical deep-dives and business-level storytelling.</p> </li> <li> <p>Builder: eager to shape a hybrid BD + technical role in a fast-scaling program while also able to get hands on and build both for DDFS and with startups using Datadog.</p> </li> <li> <p>Storyteller: able to translate technical differentiators into startup-relevant business value.</p> </li> <li> <p>Startup advocate: passionate about helping early-stage companies scale successfully with Datadog.</p> </li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Nice-to-Haves</strong></p> <ul> <li> <p>Strong knowledge of Datadog’s product suite and observability/security ecosystem.</p> </li> <li> <p>A former technical founder or engineering leader at a startup.</p> </li> <li> <p>Experience running or speaking at startup/technical events.</p> </li> <li> <p>Background in developer advocacy, community building, or technical marketing.</p> </li> <li> <p>Knowledge of VC/accelerator ecosystems and startup go-to-market motion.</p> </li> <li> <p>Hands-on ability to articulate Datadog’s differentiators against competitors (Grafana, Sentry, Clickhouse, New Relic, etc.)</p> </li> <li> <p>CRM proficiency (Salesforce, HubSpot) and ability to manage demand-generation workflows.</p> </li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Why Join Us:</strong></p> <ul> <li> <p>Be at the forefront of Datadog’s engagement with the global startup ecosystem.</p> </li> <li> <p>Shape a high-impact program where technical expertise and community growth intersect.</p> </li> <li> <p>Help the next wave of unicorns adopt world-class observability, security, and performance tooling from day one.</p> </li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li> <p>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</p> </li> <li> <p>Intradepartmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking</p> </li> <li> <p>An inclusive company culture, including Community Guilds (employee resource groups)</p> </li> <li> <p>Access to Inclusion Talks and internal panel discussions</p> </li> <li> <p>Free, global Spring Health benefits for employees and dependents age 6+</p> </li> <li> <p>Competitive global benefits and giving programs</p> </li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed jobs.title: GTM Strategy/Operations Associate - Dublin
jobs.description: <p>Datadog’s EMEA GTM Strategy &amp; Operations team is responsible for both strategic planning and day‑to‑day operational excellence across the region. The team uncovers, analyzes, and prioritizes improvement opportunities that drive incremental revenue and operational efficiency. We build business cases for EMEA investment, optimize resources during annual planning, and collaborate closely with global teams to enhance systems, tools, programs, and processes. We also lead the execution of global initiatives within EMEA and share best practices across regions to accelerate global scale.</p> <p>As part of the EMEA GTM Strategy &amp; Operations team, you’ll work with sales leaders and global partners to improve how we plan, execute, and automate core GTM processes across the region. You’ll be AI‑native in how you work, using modern AI tools to get to insight faster, diagnose root causes, and free your time for driving change and partnering with the business.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture – the relationships and collaboration it builds, and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work‑life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do</strong></p> <ul> <li>Build strong relationships with local, regional, and global cross‑functional stakeholders to enhance GTM strategy and operations for EMEA, sharing learnings and best practices across markets.</li> <li>Localize and execute global GTM policies and sales programs (for example: pipeline generation programs, product plays, territory deep dives), ensuring they are adapted effectively to EMEA market realities.</li> <li>Act as a primary point of contact for a defined set of sales leaders on strategic and operational questions (e.g. account coverage, rules of engagement, data led operating cadence, QBR inputs etc..).</li> <li>Orchestrate and support the rhythm of business, including data preparation and standardization for Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs), forecast/LQSR cadences, and annual planning cycles.</li> <li>Partner with systems, data, and central operations teams to simplify and automate recurring work (for example: standard QBR/LQSR kits, self‑serve lists and dashboards, queue and SLA reporting), using AI‑enabled workflows where it meaningfully reduces manual effort.</li> <li>Produce analyses, reports, and recommendations on sales performance, coverage, pipeline, and program effectiveness, leveraging AI tools to explore data faster, spot patterns, and get to root causes so you can focus more time on driving action with stakeholders.</li> <li>Manage cross‑functional projects from initiation to completion, ensuring alignment with strategic objectives, clear timelines, and well‑defined success metrics.</li> <li>Document processes, definitions, and best practices so that outputs are scalable, repeatable, and easy for sales leaders and partner teams to adopt.</li> </ul> <p>Who You Are</p> <ul> <li>2–4+ years of experience in GTM Strategy &amp; Operations, management consulting, banking, analytics, or a similar analytical field, ideally within SaaS or high‑growth B2B technology.</li> <li>Experience working with go‑to‑market teams (e.g. Sales, CS, Marketing) and comfort acting as a trusted advisor to commercial leadership.</li> <li>Strong analytical skills and ability to translate data into clear insights and practical recommendations that improve business performance.</li> <li>Comfort using modern AI tools as part of your day‑to‑day workflow (for example, to accelerate analysis, draft and refine narratives, or prototype simple automations), and curiosity to experiment with new capabilities.</li> <li>Familiarity with Salesforce and BI tools (e.g. Tableau, Metabase, or similar); experience with SQL or a willingness to learn on the job.</li> <li>High attention to detail, strong organizational skills, and the ability to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders in a fast‑moving environment.</li> <li>Comfortable operating in ambiguity and shifting between big‑picture strategic thinking and hands‑on execution.</li> <li>Effective verbal and written communicator who can build alignment and influence cross‑functional partners.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-NG2</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. jobs.title: IT Support Technician Intern
jobs.description: <p>We're looking for IT support interns to join Datadog's Enterprise IT organization — the team responsible for managing the internal tools, systems, and services that keep Datadog running smoothly as we scale. As part of this team, you'll work alongside IT professionals to troubleshoot issues, support employee onboarding and offboarding, manage SaaS applications, and help maintain the infrastructure that internal teams rely on every day. This means providing excellent customer service to our employees to support thousands of endpoint devices such as macOS, iOS, Chrome OS and Windows. In addition, you will support software as a service (SaaS) solutions like Google Workspace, Slack, Zoom, Jamf, and more.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>About the Role</strong></p> <p>The Enterprise IT Support team is responsible for the hardware and software that Datadog employees around the world use on a daily basis to successfully do their jobs. As an Enterprise IT Support intern, you will combine theoretical learning at your school with practical, hands-on experience in our IT Support team. Over the course of your internship, you will progressively take on more responsibilities while helping ensure our ever-growing user base has everything they need to do their job.&nbsp;</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships that it builds, the creativity it brings to the table, and the collaboration of being together. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our employees can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p style="line-height: 2;"><strong><br></strong><strong>What You'll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Learn and assist in providing customer service and technical support for our employees</li> <li>Shadow and support laptop deployment as part of onboarding and refresh cycles</li> <li>Participate in new hire orientations to understand policies, best practices and services used within Datadog</li> <li>Assist with general ticketing, account management, and basic troubleshooting under supervision</li> <li>Help maintain hardware and software inventory</li> <li>Contribute to documentation creation and maintenance</li> <li>Observe and support unified <li>Develop skills in various SaaS tools and IT workflows</li> </ul> <p><strong>&nbsp;</strong></p> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>You will graduate in 2026 with a degree in IT, This role offers the opportunity to lead a future-facing API platform at the intersection of distributed systems, developer experience, and AI-powered product development. The person in this role will help define how Datadog exposes APM intelligence across MCP tools, skills, CLI interfaces, and other emerging surfaces as the ecosystem evolves.<br><br></p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. jobs.title: Manager I, Engineering - Applied AI - Natural Language & Conversational Interfaces
jobs.description: <p>Datadog's Applied AI group is building the AI capabilities that transform how users interact with our platform. Our Natural Language Query (NLQ) and Command-I (Cmd-I) teams are at the forefront of this transformation, enabling users to query telemetry data, investigate production issues, and configure Datadog through natural language and intelligent conversational agents.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>As an <strong>Engineering Manager I</strong> in Applied AI, you will lead a team of engineers and applied scientists building natural language interfaces and conversational AI systems. Your teams will work on challenges spanning LLM-powered query translation, context-aware retrieval systems, agentic architectures, and evaluation frameworks. You will guide technical execution, support team growth, and collaborate across engineering, product, and research to deliver AI experiences that fundamentally change how customers use Datadog.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture, the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You'll Do</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead and develop a team of engineers and applied scientists building NLQ translation systems, conversational agents, or AI-powered interfaces</li> <li>Own the delivery of high-quality natural language capabilities, from semantic understanding and contextual retrieval to query generation and agentic reasoning</li> <li>Build products that create data flywheels: design systems that capture user intent and feedback to continuously improve model quality and train differentiating capabilities</li> <li>Drive evaluation and iteration practices for AI systems, including building offline and online evaluation pipelines to measure quality and detect drift</li> <li>Partner with product managers and cross-functional teams to expand AI capabilities across the Datadog platform</li> <li>Navigate the unique challenges of shipping LLM-powered products: balancing accuracy, latency, cost, and safety considerations</li> <li>Support career growth for engineers through coaching, feedback, and fostering a culture of <p><strong>Who You Are</strong></p> <ul> <li>A technical leader with experience in AI, machine learning, or NLP systems</li> <li>Proven experience building and shipping LLM-powered products, conversational AI, or natural language interfaces</li> <li>A product builder who thinks beyond features, designing systems that generate data, learn from usage, and compound in value over time</li> jobs.description: <p>Datadog's Applied AI group builds AI foundations and AI-powered features across the Datadog platform. We train ML models, build agents, and ship natural language interfaces that help thousands of customers detect problems, investigate incidents, and act on their infrastructure. Our teams work with some of the world's richest observability data, hundreds of trillions of data points per day, to build production AI at real scale.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>Our work spans ML models, agentic systems, conversational AI, foundation models, and the infrastructure that supports them. As a Manager II, you will lead multiple teams of engineers, applied scientists, and managers in this space. You own the technical direction and the team culture. You drive work from research through production, partner with product to set priorities, and help shape how Datadog uses AI to close the loop between detecting a problem and fixing it.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture—the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings. jobs.title: Product Manager II - AI & Data Security
jobs.description: <p>As a Product Manager II for AI &amp; Data Security at Datadog, you will define and deliver capabilities that help organizations securely adopt and scale AI across their applications and infrastructure. You’ll focus on building products that provide visibility into AI systems and data usage, assess security posture, and enable teams to manage risk across the AI lifecycle. This role sits at the intersection of security, AI, and cloud platforms, and is ideal for a PM who thrives in emerging, ambiguous problem spaces. You will work cross-functionally to shape how customers discover, understand, and secure AI-powered systems in production.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li> <p>Own and drive the roadmap for AI &amp; Data Security capabilities, including AI and data discovery and posture management</p> </li> <li> <p>Define how customers assess and manage the security posture of AI systems, including risks related to configuration, data exposure, and policy compliance</p> </li> <li> <p>Partner with engineering and design to deliver end-to-end product capabilities, from concept through launch and iteration</p> </li> <li> <p>Collaborate with security research teams to identify emerging risks in AI systems and translate them into actionable product features</p> </li> <li> <p>Engage with customers to understand AI adoption patterns and validate solutions that enable secure, scalable operations</p> </li> <li> <p>Define and track success metrics such as product adoption, usage, and impact on customer security workflows</p> </li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li> <p>3+ years of product management experience building technical products in areas such as security, AI, data, cloud infrastructure, or developer platforms</p> </li> <li> <p>Strong understanding of modern cloud environments, application architectures, and the fundamentals of generative AI systems</p> </li> <li> <p>Interest in the security challenges introduced by AI and machine learning systems, particularly in production environments</p> </li> <li> <p>Comfortable working closely with engineers and discussing technical design, tradeoffs, and implementation details</p> </li> <li> <p>Experience engaging with customers and using their feedback to inform product decisions and prioritization</p> </li> <li> <p>Ability to navigate ambiguity and translate complex problem spaces into clear product direction and execution</p> </li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. If you're excited to grow with a customer-obsessed, fast-moving Marketing team that values innovation and collaboration, we’d love to hear from you.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. jobs.title: Rev Ops, Technical Programs Specialist - Boston
jobs.description: <p><strong>Purpose</strong></p> <p>This role will partner with our business stakeholders across the business to translate key business objectives into operational processes and systematic changes in order ensure scalability as we drive the business forward. This individual will be responsible for prioritizing and identifying the most impactful asks from the business, combining business context and technical understanding to determine feasibility and the best path forward, and working with our partners across all operational functions to plan and execute these changes.&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>As we continue to grow the Technical Programs team, this individual will be key in driving innovation to execute on smaller changes more quickly and with fewer resources in order to shift the focus towards more strategic initiatives.&nbsp;</p> <p><br><br></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Strategic partner to key business stakeholders across the organization, responsible for translating complex business objectives into operational design and technical solutions</li> <li>Act as a technical stakeholder across all areas of GTM process, providing feedback and prioritization, understanding how the design fits within existing structures and potential downstream implications, and ensuring they are built for scale&nbsp;</li> <li>Deep understanding of our current processes and automations in our entire Sales Tech Stack to ensure effective design improvements, opportunities to combine and streamline workstreams, and ensure proper change management</li> <li>Own short and medium term operational roadmap through sprint planning in collaboration with Sales Systems to ensure we are serving all of our stakeholders across the business, including ourselves in Revenue Operations&nbsp;</li> <li>Perform effective stakeholder management, to ensure that final solutions are met and any adjustments needed to design are understood and agreed upon</li> <li>Identify risks, issues, and dependencies impacting solution delivery, and drive resolution, escalating appropriately when needed</li> <li>Collaborate with business partners to ensure requirements are defined and scoped, and with technical teams to validate that solutions will meet business stakeholders needs&nbsp;</li> <li>Identify opportunities for innovation in intake, prioritization, solution design and execution frameworks to allow the team to deliver minor changes more quickly and with fewer resources in order to shift the focus towards more strategic initiatives</li> <li>Develop cadence of re-evaluation of existing processes to ensure efficiency and relevance to the sales teams&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>3-5 Years in Revenue Operations or Related role</li> <li>Clear and effective written and verbal communication skills</li> <li>Self-starter that adapts well to change in a fast-paced, dynamic workplace</li> <li>Experience with a fast-growing SaaS company and ability to demonstrate a strong grasp of the SaaS Sales process and revenue recognition</li> <li>Experience preferred in project management for enterprise size implementations and
