We will work with you to provide reasonable accommodations to facilitate your participation and ensure a fair and accessible interview experience. Your request and any information provided will be kept confidential and will not impact your candidacy. We look forward to hearing from you and accommodating your needs to the best of our ability.</em></p></div>

jobs.title: Software Engineer II - Oracle Databases
jobs.location: Novi Sad, South Bačka, Serbia, EMEA
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>From Fivetran’s founding until now, our mission has remained the same: to make access to data as simple and reliable as electricity. With Fivetran, customer data arrives in their warehouses, canonical and ready to query, with no engineering or maintenance required. We’re proud that more organizations continue to leverage our technology every day to become truly data-driven.</p></div><p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><strong>About the Role</strong></span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Fivetran is building data pipelines to power the modern data stack for thousands of companies.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">We are looking for a Software Engineer enthusiastic to join our fast-growing company.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">As a part of the Oracle Connector Team, you will be responsible to help build new features of our data pipeline service, help improve our software architecture, and maintain reliable operation of the service. We're looking for someone who wants to learn and apply the key values that make our product great, dive deep into complex investigations into data replication and build a world class ELT solution for Oracle systems.</span><br><br><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Fivetran is the epitome of data-driven-development — our engineering team is focused on building a world class product that:</span><br><br><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">- Facilitates Data Analytics — join our mission to reliably deliver best-in class schemas, and clean data, designed to be intuitively useful to analysts.</span><br><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">- Contributes to our Community — help develop tools like Data Lakes, SQL-based Transformations, and schema explorers to empower analysts to choose the best tools, and to make those tools more powerful.</span><br><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">- Is Absolutely Dependable — work to make Fivetran the most reliable and secure component of our customers’ data infrastructure.</span><br><br><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">We emphasize using no-nonsense tools, and take great pride in the simplicity and effectiveness of the systems we build. Our back-end is built on Java, C, Postgres, and Kubernetes, and our front-end is built on React and Typescript.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">This is a full-time position based out of our Novi Sad office. Our hybrid work model offers a blend of remote flexibility and in-person collaboration, including two days in the office each week to connect and build as a team.</span></p> <p><strong><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Technologies You’ll Use</span></strong></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Java, C, Oracle, SQL, Docker/Kubernetes</span></p> <p><strong><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">What You’ll Do</span></strong></p> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Dive deep into data replication debugging, analyzing existing code that is in use by hundreds of customers today. Some clients are operating the largest Oracle clusters in the world.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Analyze new features that are introduced by Oracle in recent releases and determine how to develop supporting features.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Contribute to the efforts to the maintenance and improvement of existing connectors; call for improvement in all areas of Fivetran's codebase and infrastructure.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Be a part of the process to move a high performance client side service to the cloud and operate at scale.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Develop software designs and technical roadmap for the team.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Contribute to hiring by participating in the interview process.</span></li> </ul> <p><strong><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Skills We’re Looking For</span></strong></p> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Coding:</span> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">2+ years programming in Java or C languages, strong preference to C/C++ for this role&nbsp;</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Writes well-structured and performant code</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Be able to dive into code that has been previously written and suggest improvements</span></li> </ul> </li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Design:</span> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Understand and can explain how to build high performance systems and processes</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Have an analytical mindset that helps you identify design flaws in existing systems and come up with improvements</span></li> </ul> </li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">System:</span> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">1+ years of of working in a cloud environment utilizing AWS, GCP, Docker/Kubernetes, CircleCI, or similar technologies</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">1+ years of infrastructure as code on Docker/Kubernetes, AWS CloudFormation, Terraform, Google Cloud Deployment Manager, or equivalent.</span></li> </ul> </li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Project management:</span> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Able to define project requirements. Proficient at designing software and infrastructure</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Experience understanding customer requirements and/or problems and breaking them down into engineering tasks</span></li> </ul> </li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Collaboration:</span> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Experience working in large distributed teams</span></li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p><strong><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Bonus Skills​</span></strong></p> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Working experience with database internals such as storage format, compression techniques, encryption, disaster recovery, transaction management, or high availability.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Working experience in designing and developing file, event, application, or database connectors.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Working experience with advanced Java development such as multi-threads, multi-tasking, different asynchronous programming mechanisms, garbage collector tuning, etc.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Working experience setting up, maintaining and tuning production Oracle RDBMS.</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">#LI-NR1<br>#LI-HYBRID</span></p><div class="content-conclusion"><p>&nbsp;</p> <p class="p-rich_text_section" style="line-height: 1.3;"><strong data-stringify-type="bold">Perks and Benefits</strong></p> <ul> <li>100% employer-paid medical insurance<strong>*</strong></li> <li>Generous paid time-off policy (PTO), plus paid sick time, inclusive parental leave policy, holidays, and volunteer days off</li> <li>RSU stock grants*</li> <li>Professional development and training opportunities</li> <li>Company virtual happy hours, free food, and fun team-building activities</li> <li>Monthly cell phone stipend</li> <li>Access to an innovative mental health support platform that offers personalized care and resources in areas such as: therapy, coaching, and self-guided mindfulness exercises for all covered employees and their covered dependents.</li> </ul> <p style="line-height: 1.3;"><em><strong>*</strong>May vary by country and worker type - please reach out to your recruiter for more information</em></p> <p style="line-height: 1.3;"><em>Click <a href="https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1CdawUwjo1Q1B7ghZJ_owdmmHB8VtqhBu?usp=sharing" target="_blank">here</a> to learn more about Fivetran's Benefits by Region.</em></p> <hr> <p style="line-height: 1.3;">We’re honored to be <a class="c-link" href="https://fivetran.com/blog/hvr-acquisition-series-d" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://fivetran.com/blog/hvr-acquisition-series-d" data-sk="tooltip_parent">valued at over $5.6 billion</a>, but more importantly, we’re proud of our&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="https://fivetran.com/culture" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://fivetran.com/culture" data-sk="tooltip_parent">core values of Get Stuck In, Do the Right Thing, and One Team, One Dream</a>. Read about us in&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="https://fivetran-com.s3.amazonaws.com/news/forbes-aug-sept-2022-digital-reprint-10-21-22.pdf" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://fivetran-com.s3.amazonaws.com/news/forbes-aug-sept-2022-digital-reprint-10-21-22.pdf" data-sk="tooltip_parent">Forbes</a>.&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;</p> <p style="line-height: 1.3;">Fivetran brings together high-quality talent across the globe to make data access as easy and reliable as electricity for our customers. We value and recognize that our customers benefit from having innovative teams made of people from many backgrounds, experiences, and identities. Fivetran promotes diversity, equity, inclusion &amp; belonging through attracting, recruiting, developing, and retaining a diverse workforce, not only because it is the right thing to do, but because it helps us build a world-class company to better serve our customers, our people and our communities.</p> <p style="line-height: 1.3;">To learn more about Fivetran’s culture and what it’s like to be part of the team, <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xlhtp4dGh8o" target="_blank">click here</a> and enjoy our video.</p> <p style="line-height: 1.3;">To learn more about our candidate privacy policy, you can <a href="https://fivetran.com/candidate-privacy" target="_blank">read our statement here</a>.</p> <hr> <p style="line-height: 1.3;"><em>We are committed to ensuring that all candidates have an equal opportunity to participate in our interview process. Your request and any information provided will be kept confidential and will not impact your candidacy. jobs.title: AI Research Scientist – Datadog AI Research (DAIR)
jobs.description: <p>As a Research Scientist on our team, you will partner with Research Engineers, working on fundamental research problems and collaborating with Datadog's product and engineering teams to translate research advances into products.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>Building on our track record of AI-powered solutions (e.g., <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/bits-ai-sre/">Bits AI</a>, <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/engineering/self-optimizing-system/">Bits Evolve</a>, and our <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/ai/toto-boom-unleashed/">time series foundation model</a>), Datadog AI Research tackles high-risk, high-reward problems grounded in real-world challenges in cloud observability and security.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>We are focused on two research areas:</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ol> <li><strong>World Models for Observability</strong> -- Training multimodal foundation models that learn the joint dynamics of distributed systems across metrics, traces, logs, topology, and events. These models power advanced forecasting, anomaly detection, root cause analysis, counterfactual simulation ("what if?"), and provide a learned planning backbone for our autonomous agents.</li> <li><strong>Trained Agents for Observability </strong>-- Post-training models to operate autonomously across Datadog's domain. SRE incident response is our first target, with a clear path to code repair, security response, and infrastructure optimization. We build the simulation environments, RL training loops, and evaluation infrastructure needed to train agents that match or surpass frontier models at a fraction of the cost.</li> </ol> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You'll Do:</strong></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Conduct research in generative AI and machine learning, building specialized foundation models and trained agents for observability</li> <li>Train multimodal models on large-scale, diverse telemetry data (metrics, logs, traces, topology, events) using distributed training infrastructure</li> <li>Design and build simulated environments and RL training loops for on-policy agent training and evaluation</li> <li>Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Product, Engineering) to integrate capabilities like multimodal world modeling and autonomous agents into Datadog's products</li> <li>Stay at the forefront of foundation models, world models, and RL-based agent research</li> <li>Contribute to research publications, present at top-tier conferences (e.g., NeurIPS, ICLR, ICML), and help open-source key model artifacts and benchmarks</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>You hold a PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, or a related field, with deep expertise in areas like generative modeling, world models, AI agents, reinforcement learning, or multimodal learning (or have equivalent experience)</li> <li>You have extensive experience designing and implementing deep learning models and agents, with a strong background in distributed training frameworks (e.g., DeepSpeed, Megatron-LM) and ML libraries (PyTorch)</li> <li>You have a track record of impactful publications at top-tier venues (e.g., NeurIPS, ICLR, ICML, TMLR)</li> <li>You are familiar with efficient training, post-training, and inference techniques for large foundation models</li> <li>You can explain complex models and research findings to both technical and non-technical audiences</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Bonus Points (any of the following):</strong></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Experience bridging research and real-world product applications, especially with large foundation models, world models, or RL-trained agents</li> <li>Passion for pushing the boundaries of AI with a focus on customer impact and scalable deployment</li> <li>Experience writing production data pipelines and applications</li> <li>Hands-on experience with GPU programming and optimization, including CUDA</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Competitive global benefits&nbsp;</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Opportunity to collaborate closely with colleagues across the Datadog offices in New York City and Paris</li> <li>Opportunity to attend and present at conferences and meetups</li> <li>Intra-departmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, ability to join our Community Guilds (Datadog employee resource groups)</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about #DatadogLife on Instagram, LinkedIn, and Datadog Learning Center.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Equal Opportunity at Datadog:</strong></p> <p>Datadog is an Affirmative Action and Equal Opportunity Employer and is proud to offer equal employment opportunity to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and more. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Here are our Candidate Legal Notices for your reference.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Your Privacy:</strong></p> <p>Any information you submit to Datadog as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Datadog’s Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>Datadog offers a competitive salary and equity package, and may include variable compensation. Actual compensation is based on factors such as the candidate's skills, qualifications, and experience. In addition, Datadog offers a wide range of best in class, comprehensive and inclusive employee benefits for this role including healthcare, dental, parental planning, and mental health benefits, a 401(k) plan and match, paid time off, fitness reimbursements, and a discounted employee stock purchase plan.</p></div><div class="title">The reasonably estimated yearly salary for this role at Datadog is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$140,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$400,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about #DatadogLife on <a href="https://www.instagram.com/datadoghq/?hl=en" target="_blank">Instagram</a>, <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/company/datadog" target="_blank">LinkedIn,</a> and <a href="https://learn.datadoghq.com/" target="_blank">Datadog Learning Center. </a></p> <hr> <p data-renderer-start-pos="215"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Equal Opportunity at Datadog:</strong></p> <p>Datadog is proud to offer <a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf" target="_blank">equal employment opportunity</a> to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and other characteristics protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Here are our <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/candidate-legal-notices/" target="_blank">Candidate Legal Notices</a> for your reference.&nbsp;</p> <p>Datadog endeavors to make our Careers Page accessible to all users. If you would like to contact us regarding the accessibility of our website or need assistance completing the application process, please complete <a href="https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeoJeduEZ2NdZF_65uZmWUrCM-zee7yADXX1s6Xr9zAGq8iKA/viewform" target="_blank">this form</a>. This form is for accommodation requests only and cannot be used to inquire about the status of applications.&nbsp;</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Privacy and AI Guidelines:</strong></p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662">Any information you submit to Datadog as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Datadog’s&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" data-sk="tooltip_parent">Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice</a>. For information on our AI policy, please visit <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/interviewing-at-datadog-ai-guidelines/" target="_blank">Interviewing at Datadog AI Guidelines</a>.</p></div>

Megatron-LM) and ML libraries (PyTorch)</li> <li>You have a track record of impactful publications at top-tier venues (e.g., NeurIPS, ICLR, ICML, TMLR)</li> <li>You are familiar with efficient training, post-training, and inference techniques for large foundation models</li> <li>You can explain complex models and research findings to both technical and non-technical audiences</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Bonus Points (any of the following):</strong></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Experience bridging research and real-world product applications, especially with large foundation models, world models, or RL-trained agents</li> <li>Passion for pushing the boundaries of AI with a focus on customer impact and scalable deployment</li> <li>Experience writing production data pipelines and applications</li> <li>Hands-on experience with GPU programming and optimization, including CUDA</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. jobs.title: Commercial Account Executive
jobs.description: <p>As an Account Executive (AE) on our Commercial Sales team, you will assist in Datadog’s overall business growth by strategically engaging and closing net-new customers across the SMB markets. Sellers follow a well-defined methodology, collaborate with internal stakeholders, identify the customer's unique needs, and clearly convey the value of the Datadog product. AEs have the opportunity to grow their careers in Sales and continue contributing to Datadog team success. This is a hunter sales role where you’ll be responsible for the entire Sales process.&nbsp;</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Focus on net-new logo acquisition via outbound pipeline generation activity</li> <li>Become a Datadog expert through continued product and sales trainings</li> <li>Manage the full sales cycle, including technical demonstrations and negotiation&nbsp;</li> <li>Collaborate with Sales Development Representatives to drive top of funnel activity</li> <li>Strategically prospect into Chief Technology Officers, Engineering/IT Leaders, and technical end-users</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Minimum 2 years of full Sales cycle experience from prospecting to closing deals&nbsp;</li> <li>Minimum 2 years of experience selling a complex and technical product/service&nbsp;</li> <li>Curious, driven, and motivated as a sales person</li> <li>Creative in how you map and break into accounts</li> <li>Able to learn from feedback and champion a growth mindset</li> <li>Comfortable operating in a highly technical, fast paced environment</li> <li>Experienced in carrying quota, with a proven track record of success</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. jobs.title: Commercial Account Executive (DACH)
jobs.description: <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Datadog is a best-in-class SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth, profitability, and stock market appreciation. </span><span style="font-weight: 400;">Built by engineers, for engineers, our monitoring and security platform is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries to enable digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stack. We’re dedicated to creating, developing, and supporting our product and customers, allowing for seamless collaboration and problem-solving among Dev, Ops, and Security teams globally. </span><span style="font-weight: 400;">Come join the team for an exciting opportunity to learn from top-level leaders and colleagues and grow alongside the company as we rapidly expand.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><strong>The Team:</strong></p> <p>Our sales team works with a best-of-breed product that solves real problems for our customers. Sellers follow a well-defined methodology that helps them identify the customer's unique needs and clearly convey the value of the Datadog product. Whether you're looking to learn from the best or be the best, the Datadog sales team is dedicated to furthering personal development and team success.</p> <p><strong>The Opportunity:</strong></p> <p>Datadog is the monitoring and analytics platform for developers, IT teams and business users in the cloud age. We're scaling our Inside Sales team to help <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/customers/" target="_blank">IT and Technology leaders</a> recognize Datadog’s impact in their digital transformation and migration to the cloud. This is an opportunity to bring a proven product, loved by thousands of customers, to a multi-billion dollar market.</p> <p><strong>You Will:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Focus 100% on net new logo acquisition</li> <li>Manage the full sales cycle&nbsp;</li> <li>Run and partner with Sales Engineers on demos</li> <li>Use tools such as Sales Navigator and ZoomInfo</li> <li>Strategically prospect into CTOs, Engineering/IT Leaders, &amp; technical end users</li> </ul> <p><strong>You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Focused on hunting and closing net new logos&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li>A top performer with a history of success&nbsp;</li> <li>Bold on the phones, and creative in your emails</li> <li>Able to strategically map out and break into accounts</li> <li>Motivated, curious, hungry, and driven as a sales person</li> <li>Fluent in German</li> </ul> <p><strong>Bonus Points:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Passionate about tech and the cloud space&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience with SaaS/Cloud and Salesforce&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Why You Should Apply:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Continuous career development and pathing opportunities&nbsp;</li> <li>Sales training in MEDDIC and Command of the Message</li> <li>Product training to develop an in-depth understanding of our product and space</li> <li>Best in breed onboarding</li> <li>Internal mentor and buddy program cross-departmentally</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan</li> <li>President’s Club for top performers</li> <li>Generous and competitive <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/global-benefits/" target="_blank">benefits</a></li> <li>Friendly and inclusive workplace culture</li> <li>Healthy work/life balance with discretionary unlimited paid time off</li> </ul> <p style="color: white;">#LI-SB1</p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. jobs.title: Commercial & Mid Market Sales Engineer
jobs.description: <p>Datadog Sales Engineers help qualify and close opportunities with customers and partners. You will provide technical expertise through sales presentations, product demonstrations, and supporting technical evaluations (POCs). Sales Engineers have a voice with the product team to help prioritize features based on input from customers, competitors, and partners. Additionally, you will work with various teams to resolve customer concerns, escalate bug issues, and serve as an ambassador for our brand. If you want to join a friendly, passionate team with limitless potential, we’d love to meet you!</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Partner with the sales team to articulate the overall Datadog value proposition, vision and strategy to customers</li> <li>Continually learn new technology to build competitive knowledge, technical skill, and credibility</li> <li>Deliver product and technical presentations with potential clients</li> <li>Have a direct line of communication with the product team to collaborate on feature requests</li> <li>Help clients onboard the product and assist when they run into roadblocks.&nbsp;</li> <li>Think creatively about a wide variety of technical challenges during the pre-sales life cycle&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Knowledgeable and experienced with DevOps monitoring or architecture tools.&nbsp;</li> <li>Comfortable and confident in delivering technical presentations/demos to either external customers or internal teams</li> <li>Able to think creatively about a wide variety of challenges. You're going to find new things every day</li> <li>Previously experienced in systems administrations: Windows or Linux</li> <li>Able to sit up to 4 hours, traveling to and from client sites&nbsp;</li> <li>Able to travel via auto, train or air up to 25% of the time</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. jobs.title: Customer Success Manager - Spanish
jobs.description: <p>As a Customer Success Manager, you will proactively drive new product attachment and effective strong relationships across our existing customer base through on-boarding, up-selling and cross selling. You’ll advocate for the customer internally and focus on a positive customer experience. Interactions are rooted in relationship-management, first and foremost, while also advocating for growth opportunities. Customer Success Managers follow a well-defined methodology that helps them identify the customer's unique needs and clearly convey the value of the Datadog product.&nbsp;</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Partner with the sales team to ensure smooth transition and onboarding experience</li> <li>Proactively build relationships with customers to create mutual trust&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li>Successfully carry-out the full-sales cycle for any growth opportunities in existing customer accounts</li> <li>Act as advisor to customers to ensure they’re leveraging the Datadog solution effectively</li> <li>Monitor and identify usage trends to uncover renewal risks and promote greater adoption rates</li> <li>Collaborate cross-functionally across internal Datadog teams (Support, Product, Finance, and Legal)</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>A strong communicator with excellent attention to detail</li> <li>Fluency in the Spanish language</li> <li>Experience within Spain/Iberia Markets</li> <li>Motivated by building long-term and long-lasting relationships</li> <li>Driven by the opportunity to make a visible impact with your work</li> <li>Customer-minded, always aiming to provide the most value possible</li> <li>Excited by working in an ever-evolving and fluid solution focused environment</li> <li>Experienced in negotiating contract terms and overcoming pricing objections without direct guidance for up-sell and cross sell activities</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. jobs.title: Enterprise Sales Engineer
jobs.description: <p>As an Enterprise Sales Engineer, you will provide technical expertise through sales presentations, product demonstrations, and supporting technical evaluations (POVs). Sales Engineers help qualify and close opportunities with customers and partners and have a voice with the product team to help prioritize features based on input from customers, competitors, and partners.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture—the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Partner with the Sales team to articulate the overall Datadog value proposition, vision and strategy to customers</li> <li>Own technical engagement with customers during the trial phase. Communicate Datadog’s value based on activities and work with customers on any identified issues or concerns to successful conclusion</li> <li>Technically close complex opportunities through advanced competitive knowledge, technical skill, and credibility</li> <li>Deliver product and technical briefings / presentations to potential clients</li> <li>Maintain accurate notes and feedback in CRM regarding customer input both wins and losses</li> <li>Proactively engage and communicate with customers and Datadog business/technical teams regarding product feedback and competitive landscape</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;<strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Passionate about educating customers on observability risks that are meaningful to their business, and able to build and execute an evaluation plan with a customer</li> <li>Someone with strong written and oral communication skills. This role requires an ability to understand and articulate both the business benefits (value proposition) and technical advantages of our offering</li> <li>Experienced in programming/scripting with any of the following: Java, Python, Ruby, Go, Node.JS, PHP, and .NET etc.</li> <li>Someone with a minimum of 3+ years in a Sales Engineering or DevOps Engineering role</li> <li>Able to sit up to 4 hours, traveling to and from client sites&nbsp;</li> <li>Able to travel via auto, train or air up to 45% of the time</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Best-in-breed onboarding</li> <li>Generous global benefits</li> <li>Intra-departmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, able to join our Community Guilds and Inclusion Talks</li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. jobs.title: Enterprise Sales Engineer (Bahasa Indonesia speaking)
jobs.description: <p><strong>Enterprise Sales Engineer</strong></p> <p>As an Enterprise Sales Engineer, you will provide technical expertise through sales presentations, product demonstrations, and supporting technical evaluations (POVs). Sales Engineers help qualify and close opportunities with customers and partners and have a voice with the product team to help prioritize features based on input from customers, competitors, and partners.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Partner with the Sales team to articulate the overall Datadog value proposition, vision and strategy to customers</li> <li>Own technical engagement with customers during the trial phase. Communicate Datadog’s value based on activities and work with customers on any identified issues or concerns to successful conclusion</li> <li>Technically close complex opportunities through advanced competitive knowledge, technical skill, and credibility</li> <li>Deliver product and technical briefings / presentations to potential clients</li> <li>Maintain accurate notes and feedback in CRM regarding customer input both wins and losses</li> <li>Proactively engage and communicate with customers and Datadog business/technical teams regarding product feedback and competitive landscape</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Passionate about educating customers on observability risks that are meaningful to their business, and able to build and execute an evaluation plan with a customer</li> <li>Someone with strong written and oral communication skills. This role requires an ability to understand and articulate both the business benefits (value proposition) and technical advantages of our offering</li> <li>Experienced in programming/scripting with any of the following: Java, Python, Ruby, Go, Node.JS, PHP, and .NET etc.</li> <li>Someone with a minimum of 3+ years in a Sales Engineering or DevOps Engineering role</li> <li>You have business-level fluency, both in verbal and written communication, in Bahasa Indonesia as you will be working with customers speaking this language and you are comfortable in English as this is our company's day to day communication language.</li> <li>Able to sit up to 4 hours, traveling to and from client sites&nbsp;</li> <li>Able to travel via auto, train or air up to 45% of the time</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. jobs.title: Incident and Escalations Manager 3 - Sao Paulo
jobs.description: <p>The Incident and Escalation Management team (IEM) is part of Datadog's Global Support Engineering (GSE) organization. The purpose of IEM is to continuously improve Datadog's overall customer experience during incidents and critical moments. Datadog Global Support Engineering (GSE) is looking for experts with a background in Incident Management and Escalations handling to join our team to provide fast incident response and stakeholder ownership. In this position, you will play a role in providing communication, technical engagement and incident management for our global customers. You will also implement processes based on the lessons learned from each incident to limit further occurrence. <br><br><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Drive the resolution of incidents and escalations while providing response and/or management to customers</li> <li>Design and implement real-time and proactive monitoring of customer's infrastructures</li> <li>Prioritize, manage, and own customer issues from start to finish</li> <li>Monitor and manage communications (sometimes public, sensitive and large scale) during incidents</li> <li>Collaborate with stakeholders across Datadog and its partners worldwide to enhance customer experience&nbsp;</li> <li>Lead projects to deliver operational improvements as Datadog evolves</li> <li>Create and review documentation as well as incident and escalation training</li> <li>Identify and troubleshoot recurring cloud infrastructure issues</li> <li>Hire and mentor stakeholders and peers in incident and escalation management&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>3+ years demonstrable Incident Manager experience (escalations is a plus)</li> <li>1+ year of experience in Support, Network, Solutions Architecture, or similar IT role</li> <li>Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Science/Technology, Engineering or equivalent experience</li> <li>Familiarity with Cloud computing, experience with Python, JavaScript or shell scripting</li> <li>Ability to correlate behaviors based on known interdependencies&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience driving projects from conception to delivery, demonstrated problem-solving experience, and ability to work in challenging fast-paced environments</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. jobs.title: IT Support Technician Intern
jobs.description: <p>We're looking for IT support interns to join Datadog's Enterprise IT organization — the team responsible for managing the internal tools, systems, and services that keep Datadog running smoothly as we scale. As part of this team, you'll work alongside IT professionals to troubleshoot issues, support employee onboarding and offboarding, manage SaaS applications, and help maintain the infrastructure that internal teams rely on every day. This means providing excellent customer service to our employees to support thousands of endpoint devices such as macOS, iOS, Chrome OS and Windows. In addition, you will support software as a service (SaaS) solutions like Google Workspace, Slack, Zoom, Jamf, and more.</p> <p><br><strong>About the Role</strong><strong><br></strong>The Enterprise IT Support team is responsible for the hardware and software that Datadog employees around the world use on a daily basis to successfully do their jobs. As an Enterprise IT Support intern, you will combine theoretical learning at your school with practical, hands-on experience in our IT Support team. Over the course of your internship, you will progressively take on more responsibilities while helping ensure our ever-growing user base has everything they need to do their job.&nbsp;</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships that it builds, the creativity it brings to the table, and the collaboration of being together. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our employees can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Learn and assist in providing customer service and technical support for our employees</li> <li>Shadow and support laptop deployment as part of onboarding and refresh cycles</li> <li>Participate in new hire orientations to understand policies, best practices and services used within Datadog</li> <li>Assist with general ticketing, account management, and basic troubleshooting under supervision</li> <li>Help maintain hardware and software inventory</li> <li>Contribute to documentation creation and maintenance</li> <li>Observe and support unified communications setups in conference rooms and common areas</li> <li>Participate in team meetings and knowledge-sharing sessions</li> <li>Gain exposure to deployment and security-related projects</li> <li>Develop skills in various SaaS tools and IT workflows</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>Who You Are</strong></h3> <ul> <li>You are targeting a 2027 full-time start date</li> <li>You are in the process of receiving/have a degree in IT, Communications, or a related field; or have equivalent experience</li> <li>You are passionate about technology and eager to learn</li> <li>You love working in a team and helping people</li> <li>You have a basic understanding of computer hardware and software</li> <li>You are curious about how things work and enjoy problem-solving</li> <li>You want to gain experience in a fast-paced, high-growth startup environment</li> <li>You have a positive attitude and are ready to learn from experienced team members</li> <li>Familiarity with macOS, iOS, or other Apple devices</li> <li>Basic knowledge of Google Workspace or other SaaS applications</li> <li>You are available to work full-time from the Datadog Boston office from following period:&nbsp;</li> <ul> <li>June 1, 2026 - August 21, 2026</li> </ul> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.&nbsp;</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Opportunities to work with teammates in-office and across the globe on meaningful challenges that will keep you thinking</li> <li>Intradepartmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking</li> <li>In-person intern cohort and monthly company social events</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, ability to join our Community Guilds (Datadog employee resource groups)</li> <li>Relocation support and travel benefits</li> <li>Unlimited snacks and drinks in our office; catered lunches on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday</li> <li>Beautiful offices in convenient locations</li> </ul> <p>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>This job is available in various departments within our company; to conform to US export control regulations, some of these roles may require candidates to be eligible for any required authorizations from the US government.</p> <p><strong>If possible, please apply using your personal email address instead of your university email address.</strong></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>Datadog offers a competitive salary for this role and may include additional compensation elements. Actual compensation is based on factors such as the candidate's skills, qualifications, and experience. In addition, Datadog offers a wide range of best in class, comprehensive and inclusive employee benefits for this role including healthcare, paid time off, and benefits related to traveling and relocation, if eligible.&nbsp;</p></div><div class="title">The reasonably estimated yearly salary for this role at Datadog is: </div><div class="pay-range"><span>$62,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$62,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. jobs.title: Manager I, Engineering - OrgStore Blueprint
jobs.description: <p>The mission of OrgStore is to provide an easy-to-use, fully-managed platform to store and search data. With Postgres as our cornerstone technology, we focus on transactional (OLTP) data use-cases while providing a custom data plane and a flexible administrative layer. Our users are the thousands of engineers representing hundreds of teams producing products at Datadog. As a platform infrastructure team, our goal is to accelerate product teams by helping them rapidly stand up new features and make step change improvements to their application's performance. Data storage is ubiquitous and the service we are providing is critical for Datadog as the business scales and expands its product offerings.</p> <p>As the Engineering Manager for the OrgStore Blueprint Team, you will lead a high-performing team of software engineers, guiding them in building and maintaining these mission-critical systems. Collaboration is key in this role, needing to work closely with other engineering, product, and support teams across Datadog. Your strategic vision will not only guide your team's day-to-day operations but also influence the long-term roadmap of support within Datadog.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships that it builds, the creativity it brings to the table, and the collaboration of being together. jobs.title: Manager II, Engineering - Source Security
jobs.description: <p><strong>About Datadog</strong></p> <p>We're on a mission to build the best platform in the world for engineers to understand and scale their systems, applications, and teams. We operate at a high scale - trillions of data points per day — providing always-on alerting, metrics visualization, logs, and application tracing for tens of thousands of companies. Our engineering culture values pragmatism, honesty, and simplicity to solve hard problems the right way.</p> <p><strong>The Opportunity</strong></p> <p>We are looking for a manager to lead the Source Security team within our broader SDLC Security org. This team is responsible for evaluating the security properties of every single commit at Datadog using a combination of static analysis and LLM based review systems, as well as the governance of our source code and third party dependency ecosystem. You will own the roadmap for growing our existing agentic reviewers (you can read more about this work <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/engineering/malicious-pull-requests/">here</a> and <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/engineering/stopping-hackerbot-claw-with-bewaire/">here</a>), working to maintain the safety and reliability of our systems while the volume of contributions scales dramatically with our adoption of coding agents. You’ll partner with our developer experience organizations on securing our source code and CI environments, and our product teams on making sure that our security controls scale and that the best practices we develop internally are reflected for our customers as well.</p> <p>You’ll join at an ideal time to make a big impact as the need for robust supply chain security is higher than it’s ever been. Datadog as a platform is growing fast and is used very widely, and the continued and exciting expansion of our product portfolio requires agile thinkers with a deep grounding in security fundamentals.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds, and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead a small team, working as a hands on technical contributor while building out the roadmap for evaluating the safety of coding agent output using a combination of static analysis and LLM evaluations.</li> <li>Partner with our Developer Experience teams on the configuration of our source code management solutions, ensuring secure guardrails are in place by default.</li> <li>Explore solutions in the coding agent security space such as sandboxing and hook based monitors.</li> <li>Guide and mentor a team of security and software engineers, emphasizing career development, inclusivity, and high performance.</li> <li>Take a pragmatic, risk-based approach to complex technical problems and work with your team to produce well-defined and actionable plans.</li> <li>Balance your time between management and hands-on technical work.</li> <li>Participate in Datadog’s oncall and incident response efforts.</li> <li>Have a direct impact on users and the overall trust of our platform.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experienced managing an engineering team distributed across multiple timezones.</li> <li>Fluent in one or more modern coding languages (Python, Go, JavaScript, etc.)</li> <li>Familiar with continuous security scanning and fine-tuning SAST rules and methodology, as well as AI based coding tools such as Claude, Codex, and Cursor</li> <li>Familiar with Github, Gitlab, or other modern source code management and CI solutions</li> <li>You are able to work closely with engineering and DevOps teams to integrate security seamlessly into the development process.</li> <li>An excellent communicator and collaborator, able to work effectively through complex technical problems with a diverse group of stakeholders.</li> <li>Comfortable working in a fast paced, high growth environment, and able to adapt strategies and solutions in response to emerging challenges and opportunities.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Bonus Points:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Passionate about building products that engineers love and believe in the true outcome of DevSecOps</li> <li>Familiar with formal verification, fuzzing, or code correctness methodologies</li> <li>Experience productionizing LLM based or agentic workflows</li> <li>Familiar with product workflows, and building security into the user experience</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing</li> <li>Intradepartmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, ability to join our Community Guilds (Datadog employee resource groups)</li> <li>Access to Inclusion Talks, our internal panel discussions</li> <li>Free, global mental health benefits for employees and dependents age 6+</li> <li>Competitive global benefits</li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>Datadog offers a competitive salary and equity package, and may include variable compensation. jobs.title: Manager I, IT Support (Paris)

You’ll set clear expectations, measure performance through service levels and operational metrics, and act as an escalation point for complex or high-impact issues. You’ll also partner closely with cross-functional stakeholders to continuously improve the services, tooling, and standards that power employee productivity across the business.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships that it builds, the creativity it brings to the table, and the collaboration of being together. jobs.title: Mid Market Account Executive
jobs.description: <p>As an Account Executive on our Mid-Market Sales team (targeting customers 1,000 to 5,000 employees in size), you will assist in Datadog’s overall business growth by strategically engaging and closing net-new customers across mid-to-large size organizations. Sellers follow a well-defined methodology, collaborate with internal stakeholders &amp; partner ecosystem, identify the customer's unique needs, and clearly convey the value of the Datadog solution. Mid-Market AE’s have the opportunity to grow their careers in Sales and continue contributing to Datadog team success.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Focus entirely on net-new logo acquisition</li> <li>Manage the full sales cycle, including negotiations and on-site technical demonstrations</li> <li>Identify robust set of business drivers behind all opportunities</li> <li>Develop a deep comprehension of customer's business</li> <li>Strategically prospect into Chief Technology Officers, Engineering/IT Leaders, and technical end users (SRE, DevOps, DevSecOps)</li> <li>Multi-thread into different departments of a complex organization</li> <li>Collaborate with Sales Development Representatives, Partners, &amp; Marketing to drive top of funnel activity</li> <li>Apply modern sales strategy with real time signals and research using tools Sales Navigator &amp; Demandbase</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your experience, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Curious and motivated to understand emerging technologies and how they intersect with core business drivers.</li> <li>Experienced in hunting, strategically mapping and breaking into net new logos</li> <li>Strong Pipeline Generation skills, bold in front of customers and creative in your pursuits&nbsp;</li> <li>Able to sit up to 6 hours, traveling to and from client sites via auto, train, or air, up to 30% of the time</li> </ul> <p><strong>&nbsp;Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>High income earning opportunities based on self performance</li> <li>Opportunity for Presidents Club</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSU) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing&nbsp;</li> <li>Both Technical and Sales Methodology enablement</li> <li>Intra-departmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking&nbsp;</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, opportunity to join our Community Guilds</li> <li>Generous global benefits</li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. jobs.description: <p><strong>Mid-Market Account Executive</strong></p> <p>As an Account Executive on our Mid-Market Sales team, you will assist in Datadog’s overall business growth by strategically engaging and closing net-new customers across mid-to-large size organizations. Sellers follow a well-defined methodology, collaborate with internal stakeholders, identify the customer's unique needs, and clearly convey the value of the Datadog product. Mid-Market reps have the opportunity to grow their careers in Sales and continue contributing to Datadog team success.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Focus entirely on net-new logo acquisition</li> <li>Convert inbound leads to closed opportunities&nbsp;</li> <li>Leverage tools such as Sales Navigator and DiscoverOrg</li> <li>Strategically prospect into Chief Technology Officers, Engineering/IT Leaders, and technical end users (SRE, DevOps, DevSecOps)</li> <li>Multi-thread into different departments of a complex organization</li> <li>Collaborate with Sales Development Representatives to drive top of funnel activity</li> <li>Manage the full sales cycle, including negotiations and on-site technical demonstrations&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your experience, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experienced in hunting and closing net new logos</li> <li>Fluency in French a must</li> <li>Experience working the French market preferred</li> <li>Able to strategically map out and break into large scale organizations</li> <li>Curious, motivated, and driven as a sales person</li> <li>Bold on the phones and creative in your emails</li> <li>Able to sit up to 6 hours, traveling to and from client sites via auto, train, or air, up to 30% of the time</li> </ul> <p><strong>&nbsp;Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>High income earning opportunities based on self performance</li> <li>Opportunity for Presidents Club</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSU) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing&nbsp;</li> <li>Sales training in MEDDIC and Command of the Message&nbsp;</li> <li>Intra-departmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking&nbsp;</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, opportunity to join our Community Guilds</li> <li>Generous global benefits</li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about #DatadogLife on <a href="https://www.instagram.com/datadoghq/?hl=en" target="_blank">Instagram</a>, <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/company/datadog" target="_blank">LinkedIn,</a> and <a href="https://learn.datadoghq.com/" target="_blank">Datadog Learning Center. </a></p> <hr> <p data-renderer-start-pos="215"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Equal Opportunity at Datadog:</strong></p> <p>Datadog is proud to offer <a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf" target="_blank">equal employment opportunity</a> to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and other characteristics protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Here are our <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/candidate-legal-notices/" target="_blank">Candidate Legal Notices</a> for your reference.&nbsp;</p> <p>Datadog endeavors to make our Careers Page accessible to all users. If you would like to contact us regarding the accessibility of our website or need assistance completing the application process, please complete <a href="https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeoJeduEZ2NdZF_65uZmWUrCM-zee7yADXX1s6Xr9zAGq8iKA/viewform" target="_blank">this form</a>. This form is for accommodation requests only and cannot be used to inquire about the status of applications.&nbsp;</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Privacy and AI Guidelines:</strong></p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662">Any information you submit to Datadog as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Datadog’s&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" data-sk="tooltip_parent">Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice</a>. For information on our AI policy, please visit <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/interviewing-at-datadog-ai-guidelines/" target="_blank">Interviewing at Datadog AI Guidelines</a>.</p></div>

jobs.title: Partner Marketing Manager, Cloud - NAMER
jobs.description: <p>Datadog is seeking a Cloud Partner Marketing Manager to drive the execution of partner marketing initiatives across our cloud ecosystem in NAMER, including AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure. This role will focus on delivering joint campaigns with strategic cloud partners, shaping regional go-to-market plans, strengthening partner alignment, and managing the operational rigor required to scale programs effectively.</p> <p>This is a highly execution-focused role suited for someone who thrives in fast-paced environments, is deeply familiar with cloud and technology ecosystems, and can translate strategy into measurable pipeline impact.</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Execute joint marketing campaigns with cloud partners across NAMER, aligned to pipeline and revenue goals</li> <li>Manage campaign operations end-to-end, including planning, execution, tracking, and reporting</li> <li>Regionalize and execute global cloud GTM strategies across key products, industries, and segments</li> <li>Partner closely with Sales, Partner Sales Managers, Field Marketing, and Product Marketing to drive integrated programs</li> <li>Identify opportunities to leverage partners to accelerate deals and drive net-new pipeline</li> <li>Oversee partner funding (e.g., MDF, credits) to ensure full utilization and measurable ROI</li> <li>Maintain campaign infrastructure and reporting (e.g., tracking tools, dashboards, partner portals)</li> <li>Continuously optimize programs based on performance data and regional needs&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Bachelor’s degree required</li> <li>You have 4-6 years of Marketing experience</li> <li>Experienced in B2B marketing within cloud, SaaS, or enterprise technology environments</li> <li>Comfortable working across major cloud ecosystems (AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure) and navigating partner-led go-to-market motions</li> <li>Highly organized with strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple campaigns simultaneously</li> <li>Data-driven, with experience measuring campaign performance and tying efforts to pipeline outcomes</li> <li>Collaborative and effective at working cross-functionally with sales, marketing, and partner teams</li> <li>Proactive and execution-oriented, with a bias toward action and ownership</li> </ul> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>High earning potential tied to performance</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSU) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing</li> <li>Intra-departmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking&nbsp;</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, opportunity to Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. jobs.title: Product Manager II – Risk-Based Alerting (SIEM)
jobs.description: <p>As a Product Manager II for Risk-Based Alerting within Datadog’s Cloud SIEM, you will define and deliver intelligent alerting systems that help security teams focus on the most critical threats. You’ll work at the intersection of security, data, and user experience to reduce alert noise, improve signal quality, and enable faster, more confident incident response. Partnering closely with customers and internal teams, you will shape how risk is modeled, prioritized, and surfaced across the platform. This role offers the opportunity to influence core security workflows and drive meaningful outcomes for security teams operating at scale.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Define and drive the product vision for Risk-Based Alerting, focusing on improving signal quality and reducing alert fatigue for security teams</li> <li>Design and evolve risk scoring frameworks using telemetry, user behavior, threat intelligence, and contextual signals</li> <li>Partner with customers and security practitioners to understand triage workflows and translate insights into product improvements</li> <li>Collaborate with engineering, data science, and security teams to build scalable detection pipelines, enrichment systems, and prioritization models</li> <li>Own the end-to-end product lifecycle, from discovery and requirements through launch, iteration, and long-term strategy</li> <li>Define and track success metrics such as alert fidelity, signal-to-noise ratio, MTTD/MTTR, and product adoption</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>3+ years of product management experience building enterprise software for technical users; experience in security or observability is preferred</li> <li>Familiarity with security operations workflows such as threat detection, incident response, or detection engineering</li> <li>Strong product sense with the ability to balance technical systems thinking and intuitive user experience design</li> <li>Experience working cross-functionally with engineering, data science, and go-to-market teams to deliver impactful products</li> <li>Data-driven and structured in your approach to problem-solving, prioritization, and decision-making</li> <li>Effective communicator, able to translate complex technical concepts into clear value for diverse audiences</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Generous and competitive benefits package</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan</li> <li>Continuous career development and pathing opportunities</li> <li>Employee-focused best in class onboarding</li> <li>Internal mentor and cross-departmental buddy program</li> <li>Friendly and inclusive workplace culture</li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>Datadog offers a competitive salary and equity package, and may include variable compensation. Actual compensation is based on factors such as the candidate's skills, qualifications, and experience. jobs.title: Sales Revenue Analyst - NYC
jobs.description: <p><strong>The Team:</strong></p> <p>Datadog’s Revenue Operations &amp; Finance team continues to expand as we work continuously to improve and solve business problems.</p> <p>The Sales Revenue team supports Datadog’s rapid growth by collaborating with teams across the organization, providing commercial, operational, and analytical support to ensure that Datadog's business and Sales organization continues to grow as rapidly and efficiently as possible.</p> <p>In this role, you will gain hands-on experience managing commission processes at scale while collaborating cross-functionally with Finance, Sales, Strategy, and Operations. This is an excellent opportunity for anyone seeking high visibility, meaningful ownership, and a clear path for growth within a fast-paced SaaS environment.</p> <p>We are looking for a Sales Revenue Analyst to join us in Boston, MA or New York, NY to support our team in managing global sales team compensation calculations and new business revenue recognition.</p> <p><strong>You Will:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Own the monthly sales commission process across multiple sales segments, ensuring accuracy, timeliness, and operational excellence.</li> <li>Improve day-to-day operations by identifying process gaps, fixing inefficiencies, and building practical tracking for new initiatives as the business grows.</li> <li>Analyze compensation results to identify trends, surface risks, and provide actionable insights to leadership</li> <li>Review deal structures and financial terms to ensure alignment with compensation policies and revenue recognition requirements</li> <li>Partner cross-functionally with Finance, Sales, Systems and Strategy to support compensation plan execution and business initiatives</li> <li>Maintain and optimize compensation systems, reporting and dashboarding by leveraging tools such as Salesforce and CaptivateIQ to elevate sales performance visibility, improve data integrity, and enhance the overall experience for sales teams and cross-functional stakeholders</li> <li>Partner closely with Datadog’s most senior leaders to evaluate, design, and operationalize new sales strategy programs&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>You Are:</strong></p> <p><strong>Job Specific:</strong></p> <ul> <li>2-3 years of experience in Finance, Sales Operations, Revenue Operations, Incentive Compensation, or Business Analytics</li> <li>Highly analytical with advanced Excel skills and experience working with large datasets</li> <li>A strong problem solver who can recognize patterns and translate data into insights</li> <li>Comfortable operating in a fast-paced, high-growth environment with shifting priorities</li> </ul> <p><strong>Soft Skills:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Demonstrated experience thinking strategically about complex issues&nbsp;</li> <li>Excellent interpersonal skills with experience presenting to teams of various functions and levels</li> <li>Project ownership and delivery - work closely with cross-functional executives</li> </ul> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p>Datadog is the monitoring and security platform for cloud applications.
