jobs.title: Software Engineer 2
jobs.description: <p>The MongoDB Developer Productivity team provides a highly leveraged foundation of shared tools and services that support our product development process. We succeed when MongoDB product and engineering teams are able to deliver the best solutions to our customers through a highly effective software development process.</p> <p>The Performance Infrastructure Team aims to make it as easy as possible for MongoDB engineers to test and improve the performance of MongoDB products. As a Software Engineer 2, you will work closely with performance-focused engineers across the organization to design, build, and maintain our world-class performance benchmarking system and supporting tooling.</p> <p>This role requires a hybrid working model based out of our office in Sydney, Australia.</p> <h2><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong>What we are looking for in you</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li>A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Software Engineering, or a related field.</li> <li>A solid foundation in computer science fundamentals (data structures, algorithms, software design) and an ability to apply them to real problems.</li> <li>Proficiency in at least one modern programming language; Python is strongly preferred for this role, but experience in languages such as Go, Java, C++, or similar is also valuable.</li> <li>Experience writing and testing software via internships, personal projects, open source contributions, coursework, coding competitions, or research. You can point to code you’ve written that you’re proud of, and explain the trade-offs you made.</li> <li>An interest in or exposure to modern software delivery practices (e.g., CI/CD, containers, cloud platforms, observability tooling), and curiosity to go deeper. Prior hands-on experience is a plus but not a requirement for success in this role.</li> <li>Growing skills in clear written and verbal communication: you can explain what you’re working on, ask good questions, and incorporate feedback. You’re motivated to keep improving in this area.</li> <li>A growth mindset: you seek feedback, learn from mistakes, and look for opportunities to take on slightly more complex work over time.</li> <li>We <em>do not </em>expect you to arrive as a performance expert or infrastructure specialist. We are looking for early-career engineers who are excited to learn how large-scale systems are built, measured, and improved, and who want to grow into owning more complex projects over time.</li> </ul> <h2><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong>What you'll find at MongoDB</strong></span></h2> <p>As a Software Engineer 2 on Performance Infrastructure, you will:</p> <ul> <li>Design, implement, and test features and bug fixes in our performance tooling and infrastructure, primarily using Python, with guidance from more senior engineers where needed.</li> <li>Contribute to performance workloads and benchmarking pipelines used to evaluate MongoDB across releases, platforms, and configurations, helping the broader organization understand how changes affect our products.</li> <li>Maintain and improve existing performance testing software by refactoring code, improving test coverage, and simplifying developer experiences for MongoDB engineers who use our tools.</li> <li>Work with teammates to debug and diagnose performance or reliability issues, learning how to use internal observability tools, logs, traces, and metrics to narrow down root causes.</li> <li>Participate actively in code reviews, both as an author and as a reviewer, asking clarifying questions and incorporating feedback to improve code quality and maintainability.</li> <li>Help keep our documentation, runbooks, and “getting started” guides for performance tooling accurate and approachable, especially for other early-career engineers and internal users.</li> <li>Build strong working relationships with engineers across MongoDB, representing user needs and pain points you encounter in your work and through support requests from other teams.</li> <li>Over time, take end-to-end ownership of small-to-medium-scoped projects, including breaking work into incremental pieces, communicating progress, and delivering improvements that meaningfully impact developer productivity and performance testing reliability.</li> <li>You will be supported by a manager and teammates who will help you scope your work appropriately for your experience level, while expecting you to become increasingly independent as you grow in the role.</li> </ul> <h3><span style="font-size: 12pt;"><strong>Success measures</strong></span></h3> <p>We measure success for this role along both impact and growth, consistent with MongoDB’s expectations for early-career software engineers.</p> <p>In 1 month</p> <ul> <li>You have a basic understanding of our primary tools, systems, and team workflows, and can set up and run our core performance workloads locally or in CI with guidance.</li> <li>You have completed several small, well-scoped tasks (documentation fixes, simple bug fixes, or small quality-of-life improvements) and learned our code review and release processes.</li> <li>You are comfortable asking questions, pairing with teammates, and using internal channels to get unblocked.</li> </ul> <p>In 3 months</p> <ul> <li>You are independently productive in familiar areas of the codebase, taking well-defined tickets from design to implementation, test, and deployment with limited supervision.</li> <li>You have contributed meaningfully to at least one performance-related feature or improvement, such as a new workload, better observability, or test stability work, and can explain its impact on our users (MongoDB engineers) and downstream teams.</li> <li>You troubleshoot and resolve moderately complex bugs with help from teammates, and you routinely file clear follow-up tickets when you discover gaps in tests, docs, or tooling.</li> </ul> <p>In 6 months</p> <ul> <li>You can own small-to-medium complexity projects in areas you know well, including breaking work into incremental changes, estimating effort with your manager, and keeping stakeholders up to date on progress and risks.</li> <li>You show good judgment in balancing quality and velocity, knowing when to ship a pragmatic solution and when to slow down to pay down technical debt or add tests.</li> <li>You increasingly contribute to improving our tools and processes, e.g., suggesting changes to make performance runs more reliable, clarifying documentation, or reducing friction in common workflows.</li> </ul> <p>In 12 months</p> <ul> <li>You are a trusted contributor on the Performance Infrastructure team: your team and manager are confident relying on you to take on new work in your core areas with minimal day-to-day supervision.</li> <li>You have made one or more visible improvements to our performance tooling, workloads, or developer experience that meaningfully improved reliability, debuggability, or usability for MongoDB engineers.</li> <li>You demonstrate readiness for higher level work: taking on more ambiguous tickets, thinking about the broader system impact of your changes, and helping newer engineers onboard to the team’s tools and practices.</li> </ul> <p><strong>About Us</strong></p> <p>MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform—the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market—helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.<br><br>With offices worldwide and nearly 60,000 customers—including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups—relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.<br><br>Our compass at MongoDB is our<a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company"> Leadership Commitment,</a> guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB.<br><br>To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone.<a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company/blog/culture/employee-benefits-that-make-a-difference-at-mongodb"> From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy</a>, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys.<a href="https://www.mongodb.com/blog/channel/culture"> Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB</a>, and help us make an impact on the world!<br><br>MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your Early Talent Recruiter.</p> <p>MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer.</p> <p><em>Req ID: 4263290434</em></p>

jobs.title: Software Engineer 3
jobs.location: Alberta; British Columbia; Calgary; Edmonton; Nova Scotia; Ontario; Ottawa; Quebec; Toronto; Vancouver
jobs.description: <h3><strong>About the Cloud Payments team</strong></h3> <p>Cloud Payments is part of the Cloud Billing organization. As MongoDB’s Cloud business grows, the billing and payments stack becomes increasingly mission‑critical. Our team:</p> <ul> <li>Ingests and aggregates detailed metering and usage data from internal services and major cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP)</li> <li>Calculates and generates daily customer bills for millions of customers worldwide</li> <li>Integrates with payment processors and internal financial systems to support Finance, Sales, Marketing, and Accounting</li> <li>Continuously improves customer-facing billing and payments experiences and the reliability and scalability of our backend systems</li> </ul> <p>We’re a group of engineers who care deeply about code quality, correctness, and observability, and who are comfortable collaborating with both highly technical partners and non-technical stakeholders across the business.</p> <h3><strong>The role</strong></h3> <p>We’re looking for a Software Engineer 3 to help design, build, and operate the services that power Cloud Payments. You’ll work primarily in the JVM ecosystem (Java and related tooling), with a focus on large-scale, distributed systems that have to be correct, resilient, and auditable.</p> <p>This is a Canada-based, fully remote role. You’ll collaborate closely with teammates and stakeholders across North America time zones, including engineers in the broader Cloud Billing organization.</p> <h3><strong>What you’ll do</strong></h3> <p>In this role, you will:</p> <ul> <li>Design, implement, and operate backend services that ingest, process, and reconcile large volumes of usage and billing data from internal and external systems</li> <li>Collaborate with product, Finance, Revenue, and other engineering teams to evolve our billing and payments capabilities and improve customer experience</li> <li>Own features end‑to‑end: requirements, design, implementation, testing, rollout, monitoring, and iterative improvement</li> <li>Help scale and harden our systems as Atlas and our broader Cloud business continue to grow rapidly</li> <li>Participate in architecture discussions, technical design reviews, and code reviews to keep our systems robust and maintainable</li> <li>Contribute to improving our tooling, observability, and development workflows so the whole team can move faster and safer</li> <li>Take part in an on‑call rotation that treats reliability as a team sport, not an individual burden</li> </ul> <h3><strong>What we’re looking for</strong></h3> <p>You may be a great fit if you:</p> <ul> <li>Have 3+ years of experience building and maintaining backend systems in a compiled language, ideally Java</li> <li>Understand core object‑oriented design principles and how to apply them to complex, evolving systems</li> <li>Have experience with distributed systems and services that need to be highly available, observable, and correct at scale</li> <li>Are comfortable working with databases (relational and/or non‑relational such as MongoDB) in production environments</li> <li>Communicate clearly and enjoy working with both technical and non‑technical stakeholders across multiple teams</li> <li>Are curious, collaborative, and motivated by solving real customer and business problems rather than just closing tickets</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Nice to have (but not required):</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Experience with billing, payments, or other financial systems where correctness, auditability, and compliance really matter</li> <li>Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) and modern communication protocols like gRPC</li> <li>Familiarity with data-intensive applications, data pipelines, or data lakes</li> <li>Exposure to working with I/O frameworks like Reactor</li> <li>Experience with containerization and orchestration (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes)</li> <li>Prior exposure to MongoDB as a developer or operator</li> </ul> <p>We don’t expect you to check every box. If you’re excited about the space and think you could do great work here, we’d love to hear from you.</p> <h3><strong>Working model &amp; location</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Location: Canada</li> <li>Working model: Fully remote, with colleagues primarily across North American time zones</li> </ul> <h3><strong>How we’ll support you</strong></h3> <p>MongoDB is committed to building a supportive, inclusive, and growth‑oriented environment for everyone on the team. Our approach to benefits and working models is designed to support your physical, emotional, financial, and family well‑being, and to give you flexibility in how you do your best work.</p> <p>You’ll have opportunities to:</p> <ul> <li>Work on complex, meaningful projects that directly impact MongoDB’s Cloud business</li> <li>Learn from and collaborate with experienced engineers across Cloud Billing and other parts of MongoDB</li> <li>Grow your career through mentorship, feedback, and stretch projects aligned with your interests</li> </ul> <h3>About MongoDB</h3> <p>MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform, the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market, helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.</p> <p>With offices worldwide and over 60,000 customers, including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups, relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.</p> <p>Our compass at MongoDB is our <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company">Leadership Commitment,</a> guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB.&nbsp;</p> <p>To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company/blog/culture/employee-benefits-that-make-a-difference-at-mongodb">From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy</a>, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys.<a href="https://www.mongodb.com/blog/channel/culture"> Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB</a>, and help us make an impact on the world!</p> <p>MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter.</p> <p>MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer.</p> <p>REQ ID: 1273389937</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><em>AI is used to review applications based on job-related criteria and does not replace human decision-making. The hiring team decide who moves forward.</em></p> <p>MongoDB’s base salary range for this role is posted below. Compensation at the time of offer is unique to each candidate and based on a variety of factors such as skill set, experience, qualifications, and work location. Salary is one part of MongoDB’s total compensation and benefits package. Other benefits for eligible employees may include: equity, participation in the employee stock purchase program, flexible paid time off, 20 weeks fully-paid gender-neutral parental leave, fertility and adoption assistance, Registered Retirement Savings Plan (RRSP) with employer match, mental health counseling, backup child and elder care, and health, dental, and vision benefits offerings. Please note, the base salary range listed below and the benefits in this paragraph are only applicable to candidates based in Canada.</p></div><div class="title">MongoDB’s base salary range for this role in Canada is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$108,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$149,000 CAD</span></div></div></div>

jobs.title: Software Engineer 3, Atlas Search Systems
jobs.description: <p>MongoDB’s mission is to empower innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries by unleashing the power of software and data. We enable organizations of all sizes to easily build, scale, and run modern applications by helping them modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our industry-leading developer data platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available in more than 115 regions across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure. Atlas allows customers to build and run applications anywhere—on premises, or across cloud providers. With offices worldwide and over 175,000 new developers signing up to use MongoDB every month, it’s no wonder that leading organizations, like Samsung and Toyota, trust MongoDB to build next-generation, AI-powered applications.</p> <p><a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/atlas/atlas-search/">Atlas Search</a> is a multi-cloud service that allows users to execute complex full text and vector search queries using the <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/reference/operator/aggregation/search/">MongoDB Query Language</a>. Our users are free to focus on relevance and data retrieval instead of the machinery needed to search data at scale.</p> <p>Our team builds and maintains the instances and supporting infrastructure powering Atlas Search. This platform deploys and monitors search deployments, providing a highly scalable yet observable system for customers and engineers.&nbsp; The Atlas Search product is quickly gaining traction with customers and we are shipping core infrastructure components that enable this growth.</p> <p>This role is based in San Francisco, CA with an in-office or hybrid work model.</p> <h3>Successful candidates will have the following qualities:</h3> <ul> <li>2+ years of hands-on experience designing, building, testing, and maintaining industrial-strength backend software and automation in complex codebases</li> <li>Experience developing distributed systems and multithreaded applications</li> <li>Familiarity with public cloud platforms, distributed infrastructure, and metric-based development</li> <li>Experience with at least one modern statically typed programming language, and interest in working with Java</li> <li>Excellent verbal and written technical communication skills and enthusiasm for collaborating closely with colleagues</li> <li>A growth mindset and the desire to learn quickly through taking on challenges, reflecting on outcomes, and incorporating feedback</li> <li>A strong sense of ownership over their work, from initial design all the way through maintaining code in production</li> </ul> <h3>You will:</h3> <ul> <li>Contribute to the design, implementation, and support of projects that improve the scalability of Atlas Search to make using it a seamless experience for even the largest workloads</li> <li>Work with a collaborative team that prioritizes sound technical decision-making and building systems that our customers love and that we are proud of as engineers</li> <li>Have the opportunity to lead projects and own subsystems</li> <li>Provide input on the team’s roadmap and help determine the architecture of our system</li> </ul> <h3>Success measures:</h3> <ul> <li>In 3 months you’ll have a solid high-level understanding of what our team does and how we operate. You’ll have contributed to the development of an existing project and completed several small improvements or bug fixes</li> <li>In 6 months you’ll be reviewing code and project designs, and be an active participant in team meetings</li> <li>In 12 months you’ll have a thorough understanding of the systems our team owns and have led a small project. You’ll have proposed small improvements to our code, product, or team processes</li> </ul> <h3>About MongoDB</h3> <p>MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform, the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market, helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.</p> <p>With offices worldwide and over 60,000 customers, including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups, relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.</p> <p>Our compass at MongoDB is our <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company">Leadership Commitment,</a> guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB.&nbsp;</p> <p>To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company/blog/culture/employee-benefits-that-make-a-difference-at-mongodb">From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy</a>, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys.<a href="https://www.mongodb.com/blog/channel/culture"> Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB</a>, and help us make an impact on the world!</p> <p>MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter.</p> <p>MongoDB, Inc. provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type and makes all hiring decisions without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws.</p> <p>Req ID: 4263333191</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>MongoDB’s base salary range for this role is posted below. Compensation at the time of offer is unique to each candidate and based on a variety of factors such as skill set, experience, qualifications, and work location. Salary is one part of MongoDB’s total compensation and benefits package. Other benefits for eligible employees may include: equity, participation in the employee stock purchase program, flexible paid time off, 20 weeks fully-paid gender-neutral parental leave, fertility and adoption assistance, 401(k) plan, mental health counseling, access to transgender-inclusive health insurance coverage, and health benefits offerings. Please note, the base salary range listed below and the benefits in this paragraph are only applicable to U.S.-based candidates.</p></div><div class="title">MongoDB’s base salary range for this role in the U.S. is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$109,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$215,000 USD</span></div></div></div>

jobs.title: Software Engineer 3, Atlas Search Systems

Our product is quickly gaining traction with customers and we are making core architectural improvements that you will contribute to.</p> <p>This role is based in Toronto, ON hybrid.</p> <h3>Successful candidates will have the following qualities:</h3> <ul> <li>2+ years of hands-on experience designing, building, testing, and maintaining industrial-strength backend software in a complex codebase</li> <li>Experience developing distributed systems and cloud services</li> <li>Experience with at least one modern statically typed programming language, and interest in working with Java</li> <li>Excellent verbal and written technical communication skills and enthusiasm for collaborating closely with colleagues</li> <li>A growth mindset and the desire to learn quickly through taking on challenges, reflecting on outcomes, and incorporating feedback</li> <li>A strong sense of ownership over their work, from initial design all the way through maintaining code in production</li> </ul> <h3>You will:</h3> <ul> <li>Contribute to the design, implementation, and support of projects that improve the scalability of Atlas Search to make using it a seamless experience for even the largest workloads</li> <li>Work with a collaborative team that prioritizes sound technical decision-making and building systems that our customers love and that we are proud of as engineers</li> <li>Have the opportunity to lead projects and own subsystems</li> <li>Provide input on the team’s roadmap and help determine the architecture of our system</li> </ul> <h3>Success measures:</h3> <ul> <li>In 3 months you’ll have a solid high-level understanding of what our team does and how we operate. You’ll have contributed to the development of an existing project and completed several small improvements or bug fixes</li> <li>In 6 months you’ll be reviewing code and project designs, and be an active participant in team meetings</li> <li>In 12 months you’ll have a thorough understanding of the systems our team owns and have led a small project. jobs.title: Software Engineer 3, Search Systems Replication & Routing
jobs.description: <p><a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/atlas/atlas-search/">Atlas Search</a> is a multi-cloud service that allows users to execute complex full text and vector search queries using the <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/reference/operator/aggregation/search/">MongoDB Query Language</a>. Our users are free to focus on relevance and data retrieval instead of the machinery needed to search data at scale.</p> <p>Our team is building the cloud-based distributed systems software responsible for the lifecycle of search indexes including: data ingestion, index building, partitioning, performance, availability, and backup management. Our product is quickly gaining traction with customers and we are making core architectural improvements that you will contribute to.</p> <p>This role is based in San Francisco, CA with an in-office or hybrid work model.</p> <h3>Successful candidates will have the following qualities:</h3> <ul> <li>2+ years of hands-on experience designing, building, testing, and maintaining industrial-strength backend software in a complex codebase</li> <li>Experience developing distributed systems and multithreaded applications</li> <li>Experience with at least one modern statically typed programming language, and interest in working with Java</li> <li>Excellent verbal and written technical communication skills and enthusiasm for collaborating closely with colleagues</li> <li>A growth mindset and the desire to learn quickly through taking on challenges, reflecting on outcomes, and incorporating feedback</li> <li>A strong sense of ownership over their work, from initial design all the way through maintaining code in production</li> </ul> <h3>You will:</h3> <ul> <li>Contribute to the design, implementation, and support of projects that improve the scalability of Atlas Search to make using it a seamless experience for even the largest workloads</li> <li>Work with a collaborative team that prioritizes sound technical decision-making and building systems that our customers love and that we are proud of as engineers</li> <li>Have the opportunity to lead projects and own subsystems</li> <li>Provide input on the team’s roadmap and help determine the architecture of our system</li> </ul> <h3>Success measures:</h3> <ul> <li>In 3 months you’ll have a solid high-level understanding of what our team does and how we operate. You’ll have contributed to the development of an existing project and completed several small improvements or bug fixes</li> <li>In 6 months you’ll be reviewing code and project designs, and be an active participant in team meetings</li> <li>In 12 months you’ll have a thorough understanding of the systems our team owns and have led a small project. You’ll have proposed small improvements to our code, product, or team processes</li> </ul> <h3>About MongoDB</h3> <p>MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. jobs.title: Software Engineer, Atlas Growth
jobs.description: <p>Atlas Growth is a cloud engineering group whose mission is to guide customers through their app development journey—from cluster configuration, data modeling and load testing, to running a production workload at scale. We use an in-house experiments platform which helps us validate our features quickly, releasing only the work that positively impacts our customers. Our engineers participate in cross-functional “squads” with product, design, analytics, and research focusing on a single metric (e.g. retention). Our engineering team is part of a larger Atlas Core Engineering org, building foundational elements of MongoDB’s developer data platform.</p> <p>Atlas Growth 2 builds customer-facing features in Atlas and sits alongside other Growth engineering teams. Recent projects include an AI Chatbot for cluster creation, a recommendation system that offers tips for better database performance, and a pricing page designed to optimize conversion rates.</p> <p>This role can be based out of our Dublin office or remotely in Ireland.</p> <h3>Role Overview</h3> <p>Atlas Growth seeks a mid-level software engineer (Software Engineer 3). SE3s are solid contributors to projects they work on and often lead projects of their own. They act in accordance with MongoDB’s core values and leadership principles, and are actively working toward a Senior role.</p> <h3>Candidate Profile</h3> <ul> <li>3+ years of software engineering experience</li> <li>Fluency in TypeScript and JavaScript</li> <li>Proficiency in Java, Go, C++/C, or a similar compiled language</li> <li>Experience writing database queries, either document-based or relational</li> <li>Experience writing and reviewing technical specs, and leading small projects</li> <li>Interest in A/B testing or product design</li> </ul> <h3>Expectations</h3> <ul> <li>Contribute readable and well-tested code to ongoing projects</li> <li>Write scope and technical spec docs for new projects assigned by your manager. Lead small working groups of two or three engineers for these projects</li> <li>Participate actively and constructively in code and document reviews</li> <li>Help product management identify improvements for features you own and drive them to completion</li> <li>Triage incoming support requests and alerts for products you own, and participate in group-wide on-call rotation once onboarded</li> </ul> <h3>About MongoDB</h3> <p>MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform, the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market, helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.</p> <p>With offices worldwide and over 60,000 customers, including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups, relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.</p> <p>Our compass at MongoDB is our <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company">Leadership Commitment,</a> guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB.&nbsp;</p> <p>To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company/blog/culture/employee-benefits-that-make-a-difference-at-mongodb">From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy</a>, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys.<a href="https://www.mongodb.com/blog/channel/culture"> Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB</a>, and help us make an impact on the world!</p> <p>MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter.</p> <p>MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer.</p> <p><em>Req ID: 4263301932</em></p>

jobs.title: Software Engineer, Code Generation
jobs.location: California; Colorado; Montana; Nevada; New Mexico; Oregon; Utah; Washington
jobs.description: <p>MongoDB is building a world-class team in North America to create tooling that helps customers modernize their applications and migrate their data from legacy relational databases to MongoDB in real-time. As companies modernise legacy workloads and data ecosystems, they are increasingly drawn to the flexibility and scalability of the document model. The tools developed by the Code Generation and Data Migration team are critical in this journey, helping customers with schema modeling, code generation, initial data loads, and continuous data synchronization.</p> <p>We're looking for a Senior Engineer with a strong background in computer science fundamentals, systems design, experience in the Java ecosystem, streaming systems, and data-intensive applications to join our engineering team. In this role, you will be instrumental in designing, building, and optimizing the underlying data structures, algorithms, and database interactions that power our generative AI platform, code generation and migration tools. This involves crafting sophisticated orchestration layers, robust integration points, and high-performance data systems that seamlessly connect and leverage advanced AI capabilities for code generation and&nbsp; building a sophisticated data migration suite using a modern technology stack, which includes Java, Spring Boot, Kafka, Debezium, and React.You will work on critical components that ensure the scalability, efficiency, and reliability of our services, collaborating closely with AI researchers, product management and other engineers to design and implement cutting-edge products that solve complex customer challenges.</p> <p>This role will be based out of North America in the PST and MST zones only.</p> <h3>The ideal candidate for this role will have</h3> <ul> <li>2+ years of&nbsp; engineering experience in backend systems, distributed systems, or core platform development</li> <li>Experience in one or several of Java, Rust, C/C++, and/or Python, with a strong understanding of systems-level programming, memory management, and performance tuning</li> <li>Experience with streaming data platforms such as Apache Kafka and Change Data Capture (CDC) tools like Debezium</li> <li>Experience with relational data modeling and hands-on experience with at least one SQL database (Postgres, MySQL, etc)</li> <li>Exposure to client-side technologies such as JavaScript and React is a plus</li> <li>Good understanding of algorithms, data structures and their time and space complexity</li> <li>Curiosity, a positive attitude, and a drive to continue learning</li> <li>Excellent verbal and written communication skills</li> </ul> <h3>Nice to Have</h3> <ul> <li>Familiarity with cloud-native distributed systems (e.g., Kubernetes)</li> <li>Experience with NoSQL databases and understanding of their trade-offs is great, but not required. We'll teach you NoSQL</li> <li>Contributions to relevant open-source projects</li> </ul> <h3>Position Expectations</h3> <ul> <li>Contribute high-quality, well-tested backend code to the data migration engine and core components of our generative AI orchestration platform</li> <li>Collaborate effectively with Product Management, AI researchers and machine learning engineers and designers to build and deliver on the product roadmap</li> <li>Work to develop robust and efficient backend services that orchestrate AI functionalities</li> <li>Identify and address performance bottlenecks and architectural challenges in our systems, particularly within data flow and orchestration</li> <li>Participate actively in code reviews to enforce best practices and patterns</li> <li>Help troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues in our distributed systems</li> <li>Give and solicit feedback on technical design documents and pull requests</li> <li>Perform tasks related to process such as CI/CD, quality, testing, etc&nbsp;</li> </ul> <h3>Success Measures</h3> <p>Within the first three months, you will have:</p> <ul> <li>Familiarize yourself with the MongoDB database and aggregation language</li> <li>Familiarize yourself with the backend tech stack including Java, Spring Boot, and Kafka</li> <li>Set up software development infrastructure (tech stack, build tools, etc) to enable development using the relevant tech stacks</li> <li>Started collaborating with your peers and contributed to code reviews</li> </ul> <p>Within six months, you will have:</p> <ul> <li>Familiarised yourself with the rest of our the application modernization tool stack</li> <li>Delivered at least one large scale feature that spans the entire tech stack</li> <li>Reviewed and contributed to scope and technical design documents</li> </ul> <p>Within 12 months, you will have:</p> <ul> <li>Become a key contributor to our backend stack, capable of taking on complex features independently</li> <li>Helped recruit and interview new members of the team</li> <li>Collaborated effectively with other teams at MongoDB on cross-functional projects</li> </ul> <h3 class="p1">About MongoDB</h3> <p class="p2">MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform—the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market—helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.</p> <p class="p2">With offices worldwide and nearly 60,000 customers—including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups—relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.</p> <p class="p2">Our compass at MongoDB is our <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company">Leadership Commitment,</a> guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB.&nbsp;</p> <p class="p2">To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company/blog/culture/employee-benefits-that-make-a-difference-at-mongodb">From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy</a>, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys.<a href="https://www.mongodb.com/blog/channel/culture"> Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB</a>, and help us make an impact on the world!</p> <p class="p2">MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. Please note, the base salary range listed below and the benefits in this paragraph are only applicable to U.S.-based candidates.</p></div><div class="title">MongoDB’s base salary range for this role in the U.S. is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$109,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$215,000 USD</span></div></div></div>

jobs.title: Technical Writer
jobs.description: <p>The Drivers Documentation team is a hands-on and enthusiastic group of engineers who are passionate about teaching developers how to work with data and build applications. We are hiring a “Technical Writer, Drivers” to help us create world-class documentation for MongoDB's developer-focused products -- in particular, MongoDB Drivers and Connectors.</p> <p>Our team works closely with MongoDB's engineering and product organizations to deliver outstanding developer documentation for everyone, regardless of where they are in their software engineering career. Every month, hundreds of thousands of developers turn to our content to help them build microservices, backend and line of business applications, and other projects that are used by millions of people worldwide.</p> <p>We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Dublin for our hybrid working model.</p> <h3>The team is responsible for the following documentation:&nbsp;</h3> <ul> <li><a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/drivers/">MongoDB Drivers</a>, including <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/drivers/node/current/">Node</a>, <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/drivers/java/sync/current/">Java</a>, <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/drivers/go/current/">Go</a>, <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/drivers/csharp/current/">.NET/C#</a>, and more!&nbsp;</li> <li><a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/spark-connector/current/">MongoDB Spark Connector</a></li> <li>MongoDB <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/core/queryable-encryption/">Queryable Encryption</a> and <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/core/csfle/">Client-Side Field Level Encryption</a></li> </ul> <h3>Candidate Profile:&nbsp;</h3> <p>Candidates must have 3+ years of combined experience in one or more of the following roles: technical writer, developer advocate, and/or&nbsp; software engineer. Beyond industry experience, we are looking for someone who is:</p> <ul> <li>Experienced with developing and shipping modern line-of-business applications OR has extensive experience contributing to complex documentation sites, preferably in a docs-as-code environment</li> <li>Experienced with using great developer documentation and educational materials. You are excited to empower other developers by creating best-in-class documentation</li> <li>Experienced with and enjoys deep investigations of new technical concepts plus teaching others what you’ve learned</li> <li>Eager to keep learning and growing. Passionate about giving and receiving feedback</li> <li>Empathetic with developers of different skill levels and backgrounds to understand what they might find challenging</li> </ul> <h3>Position Expectations:</h3> <ul> <li>Work with technical and non-technical teams to meet the documentation needs of our users</li> <li>Research functionality and features and validate it through testing</li> <li>Develop sample apps &amp; code examples to help users kickstart their projects or refresh themselves on syntax</li> <li>Write documentation for MongoDB <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/drivers/">Drivers</a> and <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/tools-and-connectors/#connectors">Connectors</a>. MongoDB develops many official drivers and connectors that users rely on every day</li> <li>Develop tools that help us create and maintain content</li> </ul> <h3>Success Measures</h3> <ul> <li>Within three months, you’ll be comfortable with our tools and writing style and be able to apply them when authoring new content for MongoDB Drivers and Connectors. You’ll also have completed our company’s required technical onboarding.</li> <li>Within six months, you’ll have gained enough product expertise to propose and deliver new projects while building relationships with the teams that we work with. You’ll also be applying our writing style and coding best practices in both your own work and reviews of your teammates’ work.</li> <li>Within twelve months, you’ll have taken ownership of a subject area and be able to estimate and deliver the related work. By this time, you’ll have performed a full annual cycle of performance and growth conversations with your manager.</li> </ul> <h3>About MongoDB</h3> <p>MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform, the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market, helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.</p> <p>With offices worldwide and over 60,000 customers, including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups, relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.</p> <p>Our compass at MongoDB is our <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company">Leadership Commitment,</a> guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB.&nbsp;</p> <p>To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company/blog/culture/employee-benefits-that-make-a-difference-at-mongodb">From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy</a>, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys.<a href="https://www.mongodb.com/blog/channel/culture"> Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB</a>, and help us make an impact on the world!</p> <p>MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter.</p> <p>MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer.</p> <p><em>Req ID: 1273396383</em></p>

jobs.title: Operations Manager
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>About Flexport:&nbsp;</strong></h2> <p>At Flexport, we believe global trade can move the human race forward. That’s why it’s our mission to make global commerce so easy there will be more of it. We’re shaping the future of a $10T industry with solutions powered by innovative technology and exceptional people. Today, companies of all sizes—from emerging brands to Fortune 500s—use Flexport technology to move more than $19B of merchandise across 112 countries a year.&nbsp;</p> <p>The recent global supply chain crisis has put Flexport center stage as we continue to play a pivotal role in how goods move around the world. We are proud to have the support of the best investors in the game who believe in our mission, solutions and people. Ready to tackle global challenges that impact business, society, and the environment? Come join us.</p></div><h2><strong>The Opportunity</strong></h2> <p>We are currently seeking highly motivated individuals for a full-time position as an Operations Manager in one of our fulfillment centers. As a key member of the Flexport team, you will take on a leadership role responsible for managing operations and a team on the operating floor. This position offers a unique opportunity to invest in others, cultivate a collaborative leadership style, and contribute to shaping the future of logistics. We are looking for candidates who are eager to take on this challenge, be part of our dynamic and innovative organization, and make a lasting impact on the industry.</p> <h2><strong>You Will</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Lead, coach, manage, and develop a team of 200+ associates and 4+ salaried managers, ensuring their productivity, engagement, and professional growth.</li> <li>Communicate and enforce policies and procedures, serving as the primary source of information for the team, while ensuring compliance, consistency, and taking corrective action when necessary.</li> <li>Create, manage, and support recognition programs to foster a positive work culture and boost employee morale.</li> <li>Implement and maintain safety programs, ensuring compliance with OSHA regulations to provide a safe work environment for all associates.</li> <li>Establish and enforce procedures for building security and product loss prevention, mitigating risks and minimizing inventory shrinkage.</li> <li>Serve as a key member of the Operations team, holding the position of Operations Manager in one of our largest fulfillment centers nationwide.</li> <li>Own and report on performance metrics within the fulfillment center, including on-time receiving and shipping, inventory accuracy, and packing quality.</li> <li>Set and uphold strict Service Level Agreement (SLA) expectations for yourself and your team, delivering clear verbal and written reports on performance and developing action plans as needed.</li> <li>Collaborate with cross-functional teams to troubleshoot and resolve fulfillment-related issues, ensuring smooth operations and customer satisfaction.</li> <li>Act as the escalation point for risk mitigation and issue resolution, providing timely and effective solutions.</li> <li>Identify gaps in inventory accuracy, analyze root causes, and drive strategic initiatives to improve processes and achieve higher accuracy levels.</li> <li>Analyze defect rates, identify trends, and recommend operational improvements internally and across the network to enhance efficiency and reduce errors.</li> <li>Contribute to the development of Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) by documenting best practices and lessons learned, ensuring standardized and optimized processes.</li> <li>Work closely with the Growth and Engineering teams, prioritizing the resolution of systemic problems and driving continuous improvement initiatives.</li> <li>Own CPU and cost metrics.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <h2><strong>You Should Have</strong></h2> <ul> <li>A minimum of 4 years of experience managing a team of 4 or more salaried individuals.</li> <li>Physical capability to lift up to 49 pounds with or without reasonable accommodation.</li> <li>Willingness and ability to frequently engage in physically demanding tasks such as pushing, pulling, squatting, bending, and reaching.</li> <li>Ability to stand/walk for extended periods, typically up to 10-12 hours.</li> <li>Comfort working in an environment with varying noise levels</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>#LI-onsite</p> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-conclusion"><div id="1704749847.557549" class="c-virtual_list__item" data-qa="virtual-list-item" data-item-key="1704749847.557549"> <div class="c-message_kit__background p-message_pane_message__message c-message_kit__message" data-qa="message_container" data-qa-unprocessed="false" data-qa-placeholder="false"> <div class="c-message_kit__hover" data-qa-hover="true"> <div class="c-message_kit__actions c-message_kit__actions--default"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter__right" data-qa="message_content"> <div class="c-message_kit__blocks c-message_kit__blocks--rich_text"> <div class="c-message__message_blocks c-message__message_blocks--rich_text" data-qa="message-text"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer" data-qa="block-kit-renderer"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper--first"> <div class="p-rich_text_block"> <h4>Commitment to Equal Opportunity</h4> <p>At Flexport, our ability to fulfill our mission of making global commerce easy and accessible relies on having a diverse, dedicated and engaged workforce. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment regardless of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, physical and mental disability, health status, marital and family status, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, military and veteran status, and any other characteristic protected by applicable law.</p> <h4>Global Data Privacy Notice for Job Candidates and Applicants</h4> <p>Depending on your location, the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) may regulate the way we manage the data of job applicants.&nbsp;By submitting your application, you are agreeing to our use and processing of your data as required. Please see our Privacy Notice available at&nbsp;<a href="http://www.flexport.com/privacy" target="_blank" data-saferedirecturl="https://www.google.com/url?q=http://www.flexport.com/privacy&amp;source=gmail&amp;ust=1613840050053000&amp;usg=AFQjCNHD63svlkD-hWHlGuGOM_bSYVrnpw">www.flexport.com/privacy</a>&nbsp;for additional information.</p> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div></div>

jobs.title: Area Vice President, Enterprise Security Sales
jobs.location: New York, New York, USA; San Francisco, California, USA
jobs.description: <p>Datadog is seeking a strategic, data-driven, and collaborative Area Vice President, Enterprise Security Sales to lead our Enterprise Security Sales organization and scale Datadog’s security business through a tightly integrated co-sell motion with Enterprise Account Executives.</p> <p>This senior leader will own the Enterprise Security strategy, ensuring security resources are deployed effectively to influence pipeline creation, deal progression, and multi-product adoption across Datadog’s largest and most strategic enterprise accounts. This senior leader will be responsible for driving market expansion and establishing Datadog as a trusted strategic partner to CISOs and security leadership teams. The role sits at the intersection of GTM design, sales execution, cross-functional orchestration, and customer value creation—ideal for a leader passionate about scaling high-growth security businesses.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Recruit, build, manage, and develop a world-class team of Enterprise Security Directors and Enterprise Security Sales Specialists.</li> <li>Own Enterprise Security revenue contribution and influence targets, ensuring consistent attainment through effective co-selling with Enterprise AEs.</li> <li>Inform and evolve Datadog’s Enterprise Security overlay strategy, including coverage models, specialist-to-AE ratios, rules of engagement, and prioritization frameworks.</li> <li>Define and own the Enterprise Security pipeline generation strategy within a co-sell / overlay model, establishing clear rules of engagement for when and how Security Specialists are deployed to create, accelerate, and expand pipeline alongside Enterprise AEs.</li> <li>Define and execute segment-level growth strategies across enterprise customers, including net-new acquisition, platform expansion, and multi-product security adoption.</li> <li>Establish operating rigor across pipeline influence, forecast contribution, deal inspection, and account-level security strategies.</li> <li>Guide teams through complex, multi-stakeholder security sales cycles, including executive alignment with CISOs, SecOps, Risk, and IT leadership.</li> <li>Ensure Security Specialists are consistently engaged at the right stage of the sales cycle to maximize impact—from discovery and executive alignment through technical validation and close.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>10+ years of progressive enterprise sales leadership experience within security, cloud, or infrastructure SaaS, including experience in overlay or matrixed sales models.</li> <li>5+ years in a senior leadership role managing distributed sales teams in complex, cross-functional GTM environments.</li> <li>Deep understanding of modern security domains, including SIEM, CSPM, CWPP, CNAPP, SAST, SCA, cloud risk, and runtime security.</li> <li>Proven ability to drive revenue impact through influence, orchestration, and co-selling, rather than direct account ownership.</li> <li>Demonstrated success improving sales performance metrics such as win rate, deal size, pipeline quality, and forecast accuracy.</li> <li>Strong command of value-based selling methodologies (e.g., MEDDIC, Command of the Message) and applying them in joint selling motions.</li> <li>Executive presence with the ability to influence internal stakeholders and engage credibly with CISO-level buyers.</li> <li>Hands-on, high EQ leader comfortable diving into deals, coaching leaders, and reinforcing operational rigor.</li> <li>Builder mindset with experience scaling teams, processes, and GTM systems in high-growth environments.</li> <li>Comfortable leading through ambiguity and evolving market dynamics, proactively experimenting to drive sustained growth.</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Generous and competitive global and US benefits</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan</li> <li>Continuous career development and pathing opportunities</li> <li>Best-in-breed onboarding</li> <li>Internal mentor and buddy programs</li> <li>Friendly, inclusive, high-performance workplace culture</li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>Datadog offers a competitive salary and equity package, and may include variable compensation. Actual compensation is based on factors such as the candidate's skills, qualifications, and experience. In addition, Datadog offers a wide range of best in class, comprehensive and inclusive employee benefits for this role including healthcare, dental, parental planning, and mental health benefits, a 401(k) plan and match, paid time off, fitness reimbursements, and a discounted employee stock purchase plan.</p></div><div class="title">The reasonably estimated yearly salary for this role at Datadog is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$210,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$275,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about #DatadogLife on <a href="https://www.instagram.com/datadoghq/?hl=en" target="_blank">Instagram</a>, <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/company/datadog" target="_blank">LinkedIn,</a> and <a href="https://learn.datadoghq.com/" target="_blank">Datadog Learning Center. </a></p> <hr> <p data-renderer-start-pos="215"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Equal Opportunity at Datadog:</strong></p> <p>Datadog is proud to offer <a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf" target="_blank">equal employment opportunity</a> to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and other characteristics protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Here are our <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/candidate-legal-notices/" target="_blank">Candidate Legal Notices</a> for your reference.&nbsp;</p> <p>Datadog endeavors to make our Careers Page accessible to all users. If you would like to contact us regarding the accessibility of our website or need assistance completing the application process, please complete <a href="https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeoJeduEZ2NdZF_65uZmWUrCM-zee7yADXX1s6Xr9zAGq8iKA/viewform" target="_blank">this form</a>. This form is for accommodation requests only and cannot be used to inquire about the status of applications.&nbsp;</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Privacy and AI Guidelines:</strong></p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662">Any information you submit to Datadog as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Datadog’s&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" data-sk="tooltip_parent">Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice</a>. For information on our AI policy, please visit <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/interviewing-at-datadog-ai-guidelines/" target="_blank">Interviewing at Datadog AI Guidelines</a>.</p></div>

jobs.title: Manager I, Engineering - Core Analytics
jobs.description: <p>You will lead a small, hands-on engineering team building the secure, scalable Core Analytics Data Access Platform that accelerates Datadog’s Applied AI and analytics capabilities. The team owns the Data Access Platform — a unified interface that lets AI and analytics teams discover and self-serve production-ready datasets while abstracting underlying systems and embedding required legal and compliance guardrails. In this role you’ll own technical direction, contribute to design and code, and partner closely with Applied AI, Product Analytics, and internal platform teams to provide reliable datasets and APIs for model training and analysis. This role balances day-to-day engineering leadership with long-term platform planning.<br><br></p> <p>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.<br><br></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Lead a Hands-On Engineering Team:</strong> Manage, mentor, and grow a small team of 2–4 data engineers (mix of senior and junior) across Paris and NYC, fostering technical excellence and career development.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Own Technical Direction and Delivery:</strong> Define architecture, engineering priorities, and the team roadmap for the Data Access Platform, driving implementation of scalable, secure data pipelines and platform services.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Contribute to Design and Code:</strong> Spend substantial time coding, reviewing, and shipping critical platform components to ensure performance, reliability, and operational excellence.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Partner with Internal Stakeholders:</strong> Work closely with Applied AI, Internal Product Analytics, product managers, and platform teams to define data contracts, APIs, SLAs, observability, and curated analytical datasets.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Ensure Data Security, Governance, and Reliability:</strong> Implement access controls, lineage, monitoring, and compliance guardrails to support safe model training and repeatable analytics workflows.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Plan and Scale the Platform:</strong> Evolve storage, processing frameworks, API patterns, and operational practices to enable broader adoption and prepare the platform for increased scope and usage.<br><br><br></li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Hands-On Engineering Manager</strong> who enjoys managing a small team while remaining an active contributor to design and code.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Experienced in Data Pipelines and At-Scale Data Engineering,</strong> with practical experience building ETL/ELT, streaming and/or batch workflows, and production data servicing layers.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Practical Knowledge of Our Core Stack:</strong> production experience with AWS, Spark, and Iceberg (or equivalent table formats and compute frameworks).<br><br></li> <li><strong>Collaborative Partner to Data Scientists and Analysts:</strong> track record working with applied ML teams, data scientists, and analytics consumers to operationalize data for model training and analysis.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Operationally Strong:</strong> experience defining SLAs, building observability, running incident response, and enforcing data governance in production systems.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Pragmatic Architect and Deliverer:</strong> able to balance short-term delivery with long-term architecture and make tradeoffs that advance product and platform goals.<br><br></li> </ul> <p>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We know not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That’s okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your experience, we encourage you to apply.</p> <p><strong><br><br>Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)<br><br></li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing<br><br></li> <li>Intradepartmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking<br><br></li> <li>An inclusive company culture, ability to join our Community Guilds (Datadog employee resource groups)<br><br></li> <li>Access to Inclusion Talks, our Internal panel discussions<br><br></li> <li>Free, global mental health benefits for employees and dependents age 6+<br><br></li> <li>Competitive global benefits<br><br></li> </ul> <p>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</p> <p><br>To conform to US export control regulations, candidates should be eligible for any required authorizations from the US government. This job is available in various departments within our company; to conform to US export control regulations, some of these roles may require candidates to be eligible for any required authorizations from the US government.</p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>Datadog offers a competitive salary and equity package, and may include variable compensation. Actual compensation is based on factors such as the candidate's skills, qualifications, and experience. In addition, Datadog offers a wide range of best in class, comprehensive and inclusive employee benefits for this role including healthcare, dental, parental planning, and mental health benefits, a 401(k) plan and match, paid time off, fitness reimbursements, and a discounted employee stock purchase plan.</p></div><div class="title">The reasonably estimated yearly salary for this role at Datadog is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$187,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$240,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about #DatadogLife on <a href="https://www.instagram.com/datadoghq/?hl=en" target="_blank">Instagram</a>, <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/company/datadog" target="_blank">LinkedIn,</a> and <a href="https://learn.datadoghq.com/" target="_blank">Datadog Learning Center. </a></p> <hr> <p data-renderer-start-pos="215"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Equal Opportunity at Datadog:</strong></p> <p>Datadog is proud to offer <a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf" target="_blank">equal employment opportunity</a> to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and other characteristics protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Here are our <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/candidate-legal-notices/" target="_blank">Candidate Legal Notices</a> for your reference.&nbsp;</p> <p>Datadog endeavors to make our Careers Page accessible to all users. If you would like to contact us regarding the accessibility of our website or need assistance completing the application process, please complete <a href="https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeoJeduEZ2NdZF_65uZmWUrCM-zee7yADXX1s6Xr9zAGq8iKA/viewform" target="_blank">this form</a>. This form is for accommodation requests only and cannot be used to inquire about the status of applications.&nbsp;</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Privacy and AI Guidelines:</strong></p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662">Any information you submit to Datadog as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Datadog’s&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" data-sk="tooltip_parent">Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice</a>. For information on our AI policy, please visit <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/interviewing-at-datadog-ai-guidelines/" target="_blank">Interviewing at Datadog AI Guidelines</a>.</p></div>
